npc_shifts.direction: future leverage up; Dorothy is still acting within his promise

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made: Committed to pursue the western road in faith that Oz will keep his word.

resets: Palace corridor staging and end-of-session formation clear once the party reaches the western gate.
persists: westward_departure_commitment set TRUST_OZ_PROMISE.
Companion memory that Dorothy chose to walk by Oz's word.
escalates: Repeated trust-in-authority choices make later betrayals read as deeper personal wounds and can soften gatekeeper interactions before the reveal.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Final Corridor Tableau: Tin Woodman moves closest to Dorothy, Scarecrow's face turns thoughtful instead of relieved, and the little circle of companions becomes the emotional center of the scene rather than the palace behind them.
paths.next_session_payoff: Western Gate Departure: Session 3 opens with Dorothy looking back at the city only once before facing west; Tin Woodman says, "Then we go for one another," and the road begins under self-owned purpose rather than obedience.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: hot resolve. First image: Dorothy's shoes on the yellow bricks beyond the western gate while Emerald City's green towers recede behind her. First character: Tin Woodman matching her pace. Immediate stakes: the company must enter the Witch's country without granting Oz the right to define what their courage means.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz confession lands with sharper moral clarity; Dorothy is harder to manipulate with spectacle and more likely to frame mercy toward Oz as chosen generosity, not deference.
paths.defining_line_captured: You take the road without surrendering the right to name it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The group's unity shifts away from palace authority and toward Dorothy's self-defined circle of loyalty.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Tin Woodman physically closes ranks; Scarecrow studies Dorothy instead of the palace; narration relocates the journey's center from throne room to companions.

world_state_delta.inventory: Green Spectacles remain city-issued until departure formalities conclude.

npc_shifts.direction: admiration up; Dorothy refuses unjust framing
alertness up; trusts Dorothy but watches authority more carefully

npc_shifts.npc: Great Oz / the little old man
npc_shifts.direction: future trust down; Dorothy is slipping beyond his moral control

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices: Reframed coercive command as self-owned action.

resets: Corridor clustering around Dorothy clears when the company spreads back into travel formation.
persists: westward_departure_commitment set OWN_TERMS.
Companion memory that Dorothy denied Oz moral ownership of the quest.
escalates: Repeated self-owned defiance makes companions bond more tightly to Dorothy and makes formal authorities respond more stiffly.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Final Corridor Tableau: no one cheers, but Scarecrow nods once, Tin Woodman softens, and the Lion accepts the next step without asking for more; the silence becomes practical rather than despairing.
paths.next_session_payoff: Western Gate Departure: Session 3 opens with Dorothy noticing the road, the weather, and the gatekeeper's last instructions more than Emerald City's grandeur; Scarecrow says, "We shall see what proves true," setting an evidence-seeking tone.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: tempered resolve. First image: the green glow of Emerald City fading behind the party as the western country opens in plainer colors ahead. First character: Scarecrow glancing from the road signs to Dorothy. Immediate stakes: the company must go west while withholding belief until the world and Oz prove worthy of it.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz confession lands as confirmation of suspicion rather than pure betrayal; Dorothy can respond with cool clarity and practical demands.
paths.defining_line_captured: You keep hope, but make it earn the name.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The session ends on a visibly durable caution: the company commits to motion without surrendering judgment.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Measured nods replace relief; no celebratory line follows; narration frames the silence as tightened laces rather than fear.

world_state_delta.inventory: Green Spectacles remain city-issued until departure formalities conclude.

npc_shifts.direction: trust up; Dorothy favors observation and judgment
comfort up; caution softens moral injury

npc_shifts.npc: Great Oz / the little old man
npc_shifts.direction: future wariness up; Dorothy reserves belief

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made: Committed to the western road while withholding full trust.

resets: The corridor's held silence ends once travel dialogue resumes at the western gate.
persists: westward_departure_commitment set TRUST_DEEDS_NOT_WORDS.
Companion memory that Dorothy committed with watchfulness rather than faith.
escalates: Repeated evidence-first choices make Dorothy better at spotting false authority and extracting practical help without emotional buy-in.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Session 2 poppy-field state includes either S2_C1 path C or S2_C2 path B, meaning Dorothy's record contains relief/guilt around Toto-first rescue or accepted delay before Lion rescue.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: In the Witch's Castle feeding scene, the Lion receives Dorothy's food gratefully and the moment plays as simple loyalty under captivity.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion adds a hurt-tinged line—either "You have little enough to keep safe, and still you came" or "You left me once because you thought you must"—so the feeding becomes a visible act of repair.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the Lion's gratitude carry one small bruise from the flowers, so the kindness now feels like a hand laid over an old mark.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion becomes more emotionally naked and loyal in the feeding scene.
environmental_details: The castle cage or prison corner is described with a faint echo of the red poppy field's helplessness.
dialogue_variations: Lion references being left, waited for, or still being chosen despite prior delay.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: After rescue and in later Lion-bond scenes, the Lion thanks Dorothy in general terms for not abandoning him.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion explicitly says Dorothy named him first in the flowers, and his protective stance in Session 3 begins one beat earlier in danger scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Surface the memory plainly and simply, as something the Lion has been carrying with shame and gratitude ever since the field.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion trust rises faster and he volunteers himself sooner in protective moments.
environmental_details: When he recalls the field, the narration briefly returns to scarlet haze and failing breath.
dialogue_variations: "You called for me when I was lost in the flowers."
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive reassurance options to the Lion are framed as reciprocal rather than one-sided.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If the player has two or more Lion-prioritizing records by Session 5, including S2_C1 path B and/or S2_C2 path C.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion's kingship among the beasts reads as a brave opportunity that pulls him away from Dorothy.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion frames kingship as proof that being once weak and chosen did not disqualify him from greatness, and he publicly credits Dorothy before the beasts.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make the later majesty feel rooted in an earlier rescue memory, so the crown grows out of gratitude rather than appearing from nowhere.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion accepts kingship with more confidence.
Forest beasts receive Dorothy with warmer respect through the Lion's testimony.
environmental_details: The beast gathering carries a ceremonial hush when Dorothy is named.
dialogue_variations: "She would not leave me in the flowers."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path C was chosen and Dorothy later faces the balloon/Toto crisis.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Choosing Toto over the balloon reads as a major loyalty decision emerging in Session 4.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Narration explicitly echoes the poppy-field memory of Toto's weight in Dorothy's arms, making the balloon loss feel like the culmination of an established instinct.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: When Toto is endangered again, let the body remember before the mind does: the small warm life in her care narrows the world once more.

npc_reactions: Companions read Dorothy's choice as consistent rather than impulsive.
environmental_details: The balloon ropes and Toto's movement are described against a memory of basket and flowers.
dialogue_variations: A companion may say, "You were never going to leave him."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow contributes ideas in Session 3 when prompted or when danger becomes obvious.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow opens the first western obstacle discussion himself with a brisk proposed method and less self-deprecating hedging.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show his mind arriving before his doubt does, because Dorothy previously treated thinking as useful labor rather than ornament.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes more inventive and verbally playful.
environmental_details: Road hazards are described with attention to pattern and possibility.
dialogue_variations: "We solved one impossible thing by thinking first."
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive prompts can include asking Scarecrow to elaborate rather than merely reassure.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If the player has accumulated two or more practical seek-help choices, including S2_C2 path A.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Later allies help Dorothy after some explanation or ceremony.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Later allies such as beasts or court helpers move more quickly once Dorothy frames a problem clearly, treating her as someone who knows how to ask usefully.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the world answer competence with swifter hands and shorter hesitation.

npc_reactions: Allied groups volunteer organized help faster.
environmental_details: Assistance scenes show quicker formation, fewer confused starts, and more orderly movement.
dialogue_variations: NPCs say versions of "Tell us what is needed."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path B was chosen and no later strong Lion-prioritizing action has yet offset it.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion remains generally affectionate though frightened in captivity scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion seeks reassurance before committing to a risky plan and briefly tests whether Dorothy still means to choose him now.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Do not make him accusatory; make him tenderly uncertain, as if asking whether the old pause is about to happen again.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion hesitates more and asks for reassurance.
environmental_details: Captivity scenes linger on distance, bars, or separation.
dialogue_variations: "You are not leaving me to her, are you?"
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive reassurance lines to the Lion gain extra emotional weight.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C2 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion supports Dorothy loyally but with his usual fear visible.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: At the first western danger, the Lion places himself physically between Dorothy and threat before being asked, his fear still present but overridden by remembered rescue-priority.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his courage arrive as gratitude made muscular: frightened, yes, but already moving.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion volunteers first in protective scenes.
environmental_details: His body placement changes scene choreography by taking the front of the formation.
dialogue_variations: "You waited for me. I shall not hang back now."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C1 path A was chosen and Dorothy later confronts Oz after the reveal.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy's response to exposed Oz is shaped mainly by her Session 4 choice at the reveal moment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Narration frames her first response as composed rather than stunned; she notices the little man, the machinery, and the broken spectacle before emotion catches up.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Carry forward the habit of making order even in collapse, so the fraud is first seen clearly and only then felt.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow trusts Dorothy's read of the room more quickly.
environmental_details: Props, cords, and screen are described in neat, demystifying detail.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's lines can be plainer and less breathless.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: After the screen falls, Oz begins with embarrassed explanation and anxious excuses.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz opens defensively—"I meant no harm by the manner of it"—because he remembers being directly called cruel in the throne room.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his first instinct be self-protection, as though he is answering an accusation that was never truly dismissed.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes defensive, apologetic, and hurried.
environmental_details: The revealed chamber feels smaller and more cornered.
dialogue_variations: "You spoke very sharply to me before."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has accumulated two or more authority-challenging choices by the time she next deals with Emerald City procedure, including S2_C3 path A or S2_C4 path B.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Guardian of the Gates remains procedurally polite and minimally helpful.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Guardian grows stiffer, gives shorter answers, and personally checks the Green Spectacles lock or return box instead of delegating.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Have official politeness turn a shade narrower, with rules suddenly spoken as if they were buttons fastened to the throat.

npc_reactions: Guardian of the Gates becomes formal and less forthcoming.
environmental_details: Gate procedure is described as more rigid and supervised.
dialogue_variations: "The city's customs are not optional, miss."
affected_elements.available_options: Some freeform attempts to bypass procedure fold back faster into compliance scenes.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: After the reveal, Dorothy reacts to Oz's fraud with general disappointment and demand for help.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy and Scarecrow cite the exact bargain back to Oz, forcing him to answer the promise in practical terms and accelerating the balloon-plan pivot.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Use remembered wording like a pin through a butterfly, holding the grand lie still long enough to inspect.

npc_reactions: Oz pivots to practical invention faster.
Scarecrow becomes sharper and more detail-minded.
environmental_details: The room's props are framed as evidence rather than marvel.
dialogue_variations: "You said after the Witch was dead."
affected_elements.available_options: Pragmatic questioning options become more natural in the reveal aftermath.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow supports Dorothy emotionally during Oz aftermath scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow supports her by recalling conditions, sequence, and promises, acting almost like a witness to terms rather than only a comforter.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his care take the shape of memory and order, as though brains were already serving love.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is more confident in argumentative scenes.
environmental_details: Aftermath scenes feel more forensic and less overwhelmed.
dialogue_variations: "That was not what he said before."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path C and/or S2_C4 path A were chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz's exposure produces disappointment, confusion, or anger depending on immediate reveal choices.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The reveal lands first as betrayal of accepted promise; Dorothy's hurt is quieter and deeper, and Oz tries to soothe rather than dominate, invoking her prior trust.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make the wound one of broken reliance: not thunder collapsing, but a plank giving way beneath a foot that meant to trust it.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes ingratiating faster.
Lion becomes more anxious on Dorothy's behalf.
environmental_details: The chamber feels less monstrous than shabby, because the injury is personal rather than purely spectacular.
dialogue_variations: "You were good enough to believe me."
affected_elements.available_options: Mercy-vs-accusation options toward Oz gain stronger emotional contrast.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C3 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion fears the western quest in general terms.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion asks Dorothy directly whether she still believes the suffering was worth it once Oz is exposed, making his fear about moral cost as well as danger.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his courage tremble not only before claws and witches, but before the thought that obedience may have been wasted.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion seeks reassurance and emotional meaning.
environmental_details: Reveal aftermath lingers on the party's stillness rather than immediate bustle.
dialogue_variations: "Was it all for a lie, then?"
affected_elements.available_options: Future expressive comfort options to the Lion become available.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens with the party heading west under mixed dread and obligation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with a tighter marching order and a line from Scarecrow or Lion explicitly treating Oz's promise as the road's current justification.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Begin with sober order, as if faith itself were something fragile being carried in both hands.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is more confident.
Lion is more reassured.
environmental_details: The western gate feels like a threshold rather than an exile.
dialogue_variations: "We have his word for it now."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz's confession is processed as fraud and practical crisis.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Narration emphasizes the moral breakage of trusted word, and Dorothy's first silence before speaking is longer and heavier.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Write the confession as the cracking of a promise she had chosen to walk upon, not merely the exposure of a trick.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow looks stricken for Dorothy's sake.
Oz becomes more pleading.
environmental_details: The room's green splendor feels suddenly flimsy.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's response can begin with disbelief rather than accusation.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens with westward departure under Oz's command.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with Tin Woodman verbally reframing the march as the company's own undertaking, and Dorothy looks at companions before she looks at the city.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Start with chosen motion rather than imposed motion, so the road feels claimed even while it remains dangerous.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman is warmer and more admiring.
Scarecrow is thoughtful and alert.
environmental_details: Emerald City's grandeur is backgrounded in favor of the party's formation.
dialogue_variations: "Then we go for one another."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz's confession may be met with shock, bargaining, or mixed feeling.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy's response begins from prior refusal of Oz's moral ownership, so accusation comes cleaner and Oz's attempts to reclaim authority fail faster.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the confession fall on ground already denied to him; the thunder has nowhere left to land.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes evasive and less emotionally open unless shown mercy.
Tin Woodman backs Dorothy's moral framing more readily.
environmental_details: The machinery behind the illusion feels tawdry and exposed.
dialogue_variations: "I never said the road belonged to you."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 3 opens with westward departure under unresolved dread.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 3 opens with Dorothy observing practical details—the road, weather, supplies, gate—before emotional interpretation, and Scarecrow voices a wait-and-see line.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Open on careful noticing, as if judgment were walking beside hope and keeping its own counsel.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is steadier and more analytical.
Tin Woodman is quietly comforted.
environmental_details: The western country is described in plainer, testable terms rather than ominous abstraction.
dialogue_variations: "We shall see what proves true."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S2_C4 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz's confession is emotionally disruptive and then practical.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy responds with cooler accounting than outrage, asking what Oz can actually do now that his splendor is gone, which moves the scene toward the balloon plan with less theatrical recrimination.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let disappointment sharpen into measurement rather than flame, so the humbug is weighed instead of merely denounced.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes wary but relieved to be pressed toward practical help.
Scarecrow aligns with Dorothy's questioning mode.
environmental_details: The revealed chamber is catalogued in useful terms—screen, ropes, basket, silk—rather than only as shame.
dialogue_variations: "What can you truly do, then?"
affected_elements.available_options: Practical negotiation options become more foregrounded.

resets_between_episodes.element: Poppy-field wake staging, including who is kneeling where and how Toto is arranged in the basket
resets_between_episodes.why: These are scene-specific visual consequences tied to the immediate roadside aftermath and lose relevance once the Lion is rescued and the road resumes.

resets_between_episodes.element: Mouse-hauling logistics and the exact rescue tableau in the grass
resets_between_episodes.why: The cart lines, hauling formation, and roadside command structure belong to the poppy rescue scene only.

resets_between_episodes.element: Palace corridor body positions after Oz's audience
resets_between_episodes.why: Who stands closest in the corridor is a visible payoff for the session ending, but travel formation naturally reconfigures at the western gate.

resets_between_episodes.element: Green Spectacles as currently worn and city-issued during departure formalities
resets_between_episodes.why: The object has SINGLE_SESSION persistence and is removed or reissued by Emerald City procedure rather than carrying as a stable long-form inventory state.

persists_across_full_story.element: Companion memory of Dorothy's poppy-field instinct toward order, the Lion, or Toto
persists_across_full_story.why: These memories define how Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and the Lion interpret Dorothy's love under pressure in later rescue and farewell scenes.
The Mouse Queen's aid is a durable relationship and later lore-delivery path, not a one-scene flourish.
Oz's bargain is the quest spine for Sessions 3 and 4 and must remain the active homeward logic until replaced.

persists_across_full_story.element: westward_departure_commitment values TRUST_OZ_PROMISE, OWN_TERMS, or TRUST_DEEDS_NOT_WORDS
persists_across_full_story.why: This stance directly colors Session 3 opening and Session 4 confession payoff.

persists_across_full_story.element: Archive entries in defining_moral_choices, promises_made, key_successes_failures, npcs_helped_or_harmed, secrets_discovered, and crimes_committed_or_witnessed
persists_across_full_story.why: These are the durable moral memory system used by companions, narrator framing, and Glinda's late-story judgment.

persists_across_full_story.element: NPC disposition shifts for Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, Cowardly Lion, and Great Oz / the little old man
persists_across_full_story.why: These relationships are explicitly ACROSS_SESSIONS and drive later dialogue warmth, candor, and conflict.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeatedly prioritizing the Cowardly Lion when he is weak, ashamed, or endangered

thresholds.at_2_instances: The Lion begins volunteering first in danger scenes and references Dorothy's faith in him without being prompted.
thresholds.at_4_instances: The Lion's bond becomes fiercely loyal; captivity, feeding, and kingship scenes all carry explicit reciprocity.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The Lion's farewell identity fully crystallizes around Dorothy's belief in him, and his kingship reads as emotionally inevitable.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeatedly meeting crisis with composure, planning, or orderly instruction

thresholds.at_2_instances: Scarecrow and Tin Woodman defer to Dorothy's first read of danger more quickly.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions begin organizing themselves around Dorothy before she finishes speaking in high-pressure scenes.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Narration frames Dorothy as a steadying center under fear, and chaotic scenes visibly settle faster around her.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeatedly challenging unfair authority or theatrical coercion

thresholds.at_2_instances: Formal NPCs become stiffer, while frauds lose intimidation power more quickly.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Oz, the Guardian, and similar gatekeepers preemptively brace for resistance and reveal more defensiveness.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story's authority structures feel thinner around Dorothy; spectacle repeatedly fails to command her or those closest to her.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeatedly choosing practical coalition-building and asking help respectfully

thresholds.at_2_instances: Allies answer Dorothy's requests with shorter hesitation and clearer task-taking.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Groups such as mice, beasts, or court helpers begin treating Dorothy as a competent coordinator rather than a lucky child.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Faction-level aid arrives with ceremony and initiative, as if the world has learned Dorothy uses help well.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeatedly reserving trust until deeds prove worthy

thresholds.at_2_instances: Dorothy's dialogue options skew toward clarification, proof, and practical demands.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions mirror her caution and become less dazzled by splendor or promises.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Narration consistently frames Dorothy as matured through discernment, and false authorities lose emotional leverage almost immediately.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting later crisis narration notice Dorothy's instinct to leave instructions, arrange people, or give fear a shape that others can carry.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Order is an act of love when panic would scatter everyone.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious, directive, steadying, respectful under pressure
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot stay active in the poppy field.
Scarecrow and Tin Woodman must perform the extraction.
The Lion cannot be fully rescued in this first moment.
Toto cannot speak or become an active rescuer.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's effort to make a plan or assign tasks with her last breath.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The flowers steal the rest of her strength before she can do more than leave a brief instruction, so the others must carry out the rescue without her.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by recalling, in Lion-centered scenes, that Dorothy's heart leapt first toward the companion most likely to be lost.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Love breaks triage when someone vulnerable is about to vanish.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot physically re-enter and rescue the Lion herself here.
The Lion must remain in danger after the moment.
No alternate rescue method may resolve the field immediately.
Toto cannot speak.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's refusal to abandon the Lion even while collapsing.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Her cry can change the moral weather of the rescue, but not the poppies' deadly law, so the Lion still remains behind until help is found.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Toto's weight, basket, and nearness sharpen later choices where Dorothy's care begins with what she can physically hold.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Fear narrows duty to the life already in your arms.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot save everyone personally in this instant.
The Lion cannot be removed yet.
Toto cannot become capable beyond a weak animal response.
Dorothy must succumb to the poppies.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's instinct to secure Toto and herself first.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The field allows only a small rescue in the moment, so saving what she can hold leaves the larger absence unresolved.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having later allies and companions remember Dorothy as someone who turns guilt into a solvable problem and asks the world for help usefully.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Practical mercy seeks means, not merely feeling.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot re-enter the poppy field safely.
The Lion must ultimately require aid beyond ordinary carrying strength.
The road to Emerald City cannot be abandoned permanently.
Mouse aid must remain within established alliance logic.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's wish to scout, assess, call allies, or use the country intelligently.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Brute-force options fail against the Lion's weight and the flowers' danger, so the world pushes the plan toward allied help from nearby creatures.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by preserving the memory of the painful pause before rescue whenever later scenes ask whether necessity can excuse delay.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Triage is a mercy that wounds the one who chooses it.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot order a full retreat that abandons the Lion forever.
The Lion must still be rescued this session.
The party cannot proceed to Oz without resolving the rescue.
Dorothy cannot safely lead a direct return into the flowers.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's wish to stabilize the party before risking another loss.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world permits a pause for water, shade, and steadier heads, but guilt and canon alike keep the Lion's rescue active and unavoidable.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making later Lion scenes remember that Dorothy once stopped the whole journey rather than let loyalty be postponed.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A friend outranks the schedule when the cost becomes personal.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: defiant, loyal, emotionally absolute, refusal to move on
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot solve the rescue by sheer insistence alone.
The party cannot remain indefinitely at the field edge.
The Lion still needs outside aid to be moved.
The Emerald City arc must continue after the rescue.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's refusal to proceed while the Lion lies in danger.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The flowers and the Lion's weight defeat immediate human effort, so the refusal redirects into summoning help rather than magically bypassing the problem.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting pomp lose some of its shine whenever Dorothy faces spectacle after having once named cruelty plainly to its face.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Moral truth matters more than grandeur.
hard_limits: Oz must still impose the Witch-killing bargain.
Dorothy cannot expose Oz as a fraud here because she does not know it yet.
Public Emerald City authority remains intact for now.
The westward quest must remain active.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's protest that the bargain is wrong, cruel, or unworthy.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Oz answers resistance with theatrical hardness and refuses to withdraw the condition, so truth punctures his aura without dissolving his power over the moment.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having later scenes remember Dorothy as someone who makes wonder answer to terms, proof, and exact promises.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Questions can be a form of courage when awe is meant to silence them.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot negotiate away the western quest entirely.
Oz cannot offer a true alternate route home here.
Dorothy cannot know Oz is a humbug yet.
The audience must end with the bargain still in force.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's attempt to clarify promises, proof, and conditions.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Oz yields only enough detail to restate the bargain, so the questions win clarity rather than freedom.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting later betrayals by authority feel heavier because Dorothy once chose to bear an ugly command for the sake of home.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Obedience can be a burden accepted under necessity, not an endorsement of justice.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot call the bargain fair.
Oz must not become genuinely benevolent or omnipotent here.
The quest cannot resolve in the throne room.
Companions must remain morally uneasy.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's willingness to do what seems required because no gentler road home is visible.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world rewards acceptance only with formal closure, not relief, so obedience becomes the burden that carries into the West.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by framing later westward motion and Oz's eventual confession through the memory that Dorothy chose to walk by a promise she hoped might still mean something.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Trust can be chosen as a way to keep moving when certainty is gone.
hard_limits: The session must end before the western quest begins in action.
Oz's promise cannot be verified yet.
No reveal of Oz's fraud may occur here.
Dorothy must still depart west next session.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's wish to trust the promise enough to make a road out of it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The palace can give her only a direction, not proof, so trust becomes a chosen posture carried into the session break.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making later scenes remember that Dorothy went west without granting Oz the right to define her conscience or her companions' courage.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: You can accept the road without surrendering authorship of your reasons.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot refuse the westward departure entirely.
The session must still end on commitment, not rebellion that derails canon.
Oz remains unrevealed as a fraud.
The companions must depart together next session.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's determination to go west for her own reasons rather than Oz's moral claim.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world still requires the same road, but it allows Dorothy to rename its meaning before the session closes.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting later narration weigh promises against evidence, showing Dorothy moving forward with hope that keeps its eyes open.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Caution is not cynicism when trust has not yet been earned.
hard_limits: Dorothy cannot remain in Emerald City to investigate indefinitely.
The session must end on westward commitment.
Oz's fraud cannot be confirmed yet.
The western road must remain the next action.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge Dorothy's wish to proceed while withholding belief until deeds prove worthy.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The road offers no more answers tonight, so caution folds into a durable promise to keep walking and keep watching.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: You make it all the way to Oz, and the answer you have been chasing is finally plain: he can send you home, but only if you do the impossible first. Back in your palace room, the bedspread is smooth green satin under your hands, and it shines coldly in the lamplight while your friends gather close to hear what happened. When you tell them the truth—that Oz will not help until the Wicked Witch of the West is dead—the words land hard and cannot be taken back. This is the bargain. This is the road ahead. No one laughs it off. No one pretends not to understand. Yet no one leaves. The Scarecrow leans toward you, thinking already. The Tin Woodman stands silent and bright as a blade. The Lion, for all his fear, stays by the door. You have crossed ravines, rivers, sleep, and terror together, and the company does not break here. At last, exhausted, you lie down with Toto warm against your side, knowing the question is no longer whether Oz will help, but how you will answer him.
session_close_design.hook_transition: For one brief moment, you let the mattress hold your weight and the room go still around you. Then the stillness fills with possibility: plan, plea, or refusal—and all three begin with what you ask of your companions next.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When morning comes, how do you lead the company into Oz's bargain?

option_a.text: Call everyone together and plan the Witch road as a united mission.
option_a.next_session_opens: The next session opens with you gathering the Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, Lion, and Toto to turn fear into a concrete plan before leaving the Palace.

option_b.text: Go back to Oz first and try to change the terms before risking anyone.
option_b.next_session_opens: The next session opens with you seeking another audience, determined to bargain, plead, or challenge Oz before the journey west begins.

option_c.text: Speak to each companion privately and learn who is truly willing to follow you.
option_c.next_session_opens: The next session opens with quiet one-on-one conversations through the Palace rooms, as you test the strength of the company before choosing the road.

session_end_choice.final_line: Morning is coming, and this time the road begins with your decision.

q1_entry_point.detail: The session starts at the first true dramatic pressure point: Dorothy collapsing in the poppy field while the Lion is still being lost. No passive travel setup precedes the cold open.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the 300-word limit. Cold open is approximately 145 words before Choice 1 setup begins.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All 4 branching choices alter tracked state and have named downstream payoffs in Phase 6 and the cross-session payoff plan: S2_C1 affects Lion/companion memory into Sessions 3/5; S2_C2 affects Lion rescue guilt and later loyalty into Sessions 3/5; S2_C3 affects Oz reveal tone in Session 4; S2_C4 sets westward_departure_commitment for Session 3 opening and Session 4 confession fallout.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are clear before the first branch: Dorothy wants to save Toto and the Lion and keep the road to Emerald City alive. The cold open states why the moment matters before Choice 1 arrives.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: Early decisions have explicit future visibility. Example: S2_C1 path B pays off in Session 3 via RT_S3_LION_RECOGNIZES_NAMED_FIRST and in Session 5 through Lion kingship framing.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The designed Breath beat exists, but it is placed at 8:00-10:00 in Phase 3 beat map, not between minutes 10-13 as required by this checklist. Phase 3 fails timing alignment. Revision: shift Emerald City gate/spectacles/ceremonial prep later so the deliberate wonder-comic release lands in the 10-13 minute window before the Oz bargain compounds it.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S2_C3, and it presents no clean right answer. Values in tension: home, moral innocence, survival through authority.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: Phase 7 session close ends on a different hook choice than Phase 5/6. The close asks whether Dorothy will plan together, return to Oz first, or speak privately to companions, which shifts the exit state from the validated unresolved commitment of S2_C4 into a new next-morning logistics fork. Phase 7 fails retention alignment. Revision: replace the Phase 7 end choice with the established S2_C4 trust-vs-caution commitment so the user exits in clean ANTICIPATION around one unresolved self-owned stance.

q10_talkability.detail: Multiple choices are immediately discussable, especially S2_C2 (leave/rescue the Lion) and S2_C3 (accept, challenge, or negotiate Oz's death bargain). A friend would ask what the player chose.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned voice elements appear. Found modern/overwritten diction and introspective inflation in generated prose: 'moral weather' (S2_C2 option C outcome), 'transactional' (S2_C3 option B downstream/state language), 'anti-pomp' (Phase 6 freeform guideline S2_C3A), 'forensic' (RT_S4_SCARECROW_LEANS_ON_CONDITIONS), 'hot resolve' (S2_C4 next_session_opening), and repeated introspective phrasing such as 'the shameful relief of having saved at least one creature you could hold' (S2_C1C outcome). Phase 5/6 fail hard-ban execution. Revision: replace modern abstract phrasing with plain storybook diction and reduce introspective self-analysis to direct feeling plus visible gesture.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: Trailing similes appear repeatedly in action/narrative lines: cold open 'The red poppies brush your skirts like warm silk'; S2_C1A world-noticed signal 'like a stitch holding him together'; S2_C2B world-noticed signal 'neat and terrible as a folded cloth'; S2_C3A outcome 'strike like stones'; many others. Phase 5 prose fails zero-simile requirement. Revision: remove action-line similes and restate the image directly in plain declarative description.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: No clear instances of the banned molds 'It's not X, it's Y,' 'No X. No Y. Just Z.,' or the specified contrast scaffolds were found in the generated prose.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: The design captures some Baum signatures in the cold open, especially color motif saturation and direct peril framing, but the overall prose often sounds more modern and literary than Baum. Cold open shows 2 signature techniques: color motif saturation and emotion by plain naming. Random outcomes show partial compliance, but many lines lean into metaphorized abstraction rather than plain marvel/practical chaining. Phase 5 fails full attribution test. Revision: rewrite outcomes in simpler past-tense storybook prose with more connective chaining, direct feeling labels, and fewer lyrical abstractions.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: The 14-point audit does not fully pass. Flags cluster around Points 1, 2, 6, 8, 11, and 13 due to modern abstraction, ornate metaphor pressure, introspective overreach, and inconsistent third-person narrator distance.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Protagonist internal voice often departs from Baum's diction fingerprint. The prose uses elevated abstractions such as 'moral weather,' 'transactional,' 'self-owned action,' 'evidence-seeking tone,' and 'the room itself seems less holy for it,' which do not sound like Dorothy/Baum. Phase 5 diction drift is the failing element. Revision: recast Dorothy-facing narration into simpler vocabulary clusters from the Voice Lock profile: little/great/good/wicked/strange/wonderful, direct questions, and practical nouns.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Multiple Phase 5/6 entries: 'moral weather,' 'transactional,' 'forensic,' 'self-owned action.'
Cold open and several outcomes use heightened metaphorical phrasing rather than plain statement, e.g. 'the road to Emerald City will end in flowers.'
S2_C1C outcome and several later outcomes drift into reflective self-analysis rather than simple named feeling plus gesture.
Many outcome paragraphs favor modern lyrical shaping over Baum-like 'then/so/at last' chaining.
Narration is frequently second-person and interiorized, whereas Voice Lock specifies third-person omniscient past tense.
Some descriptive richness comes from abstraction rather than itemized visible detail, especially in moral aftermath passages.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Phase 7 session-end choice introduces a non-canon branch: 'Go back to Oz first and try to change the terms before risking anyone.' Story map and Session 3 opening require westward departure under Oz's bargain, not a fresh audience branch. Phase 7 fails canon/session-bridge integrity. Revision: remove the alternate-audience option and end on the already canon-safe S2_C4 westward commitment choice.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard Layer 3 character rules. Scarecrow is plan-minded, Tin Woodman tender and practical, Lion ashamed yet loyal, Oz theatrical and coercive, Guardian procedural, and Toto nonverbal.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible. Immediate NPC echoes, environmental flags, reactivity triggers, and cross-session propagation rules clearly show the world remembering Dorothy's patterns, especially around Lion loyalty, Oz authority, and Emerald City procedure.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State handling is not fully consistent across phases. Phase 5/6 establish S2_C4 as the session-end commitment state, but Phase 7 replaces it with a different end choice and different next-session openings, creating mismatch between westward_departure_commitment state and actual session close. Phase 7 fails consistency with Phase 5/6. Revision: align Phase 7 close exactly to S2_C4 so the persistent state written in Phase 5 is the same state the session actually ends on.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Not every significant persistent state change has a valid in-world acknowledgment. Several emotional choices use 'NO SIGNAL — state change is minor' despite including NPC disposition or archive shifts, and some significant branch-level changes are acknowledged only abstractly. Phase 5 Task 8 signal coverage is incomplete. Revision: write subtle in-world acknowledgment lines for every state-changing emotional choice and any branch path currently relying on abstract summary instead of scene-grounded signal.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All 4 branching choices have 3 defining lines each, all under 20 words, provocative, and non-spoilery. Example set quality is strong across S2_C1-S2_C4.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Each branching choice spans lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no single register is systematically advantaged in consequence design. The options read as genuinely different moral postures rather than tonal variants of the same choice.

revision_instructions.instruction: Shift the Breath beat later so the wonder/comic release lands between minutes 10-13 before the Oz bargain.
Replace the alternate morning-planning end choice with the established S2_C4 trust-vs-caution hook so the session ends on Dorothy's unresolved westward commitment.

revision_instructions.phase: Phase 5 — Choice Writing / Phase 6 — Consequence Map
revision_instructions.instruction: Remove modern abstract diction and introspective overreach, replacing it with plain storybook vocabulary and direct feeling-plus-gesture phrasing.
Eliminate all action-line similes and restate those images in direct descriptive prose.
Rewrite cold open and outcome prose toward Baum's voice using simpler declaratives, connective chaining, direct moral naming, and less lyrical abstraction.

revision_instructions.phase: Phase 5 — Choice Writing / Voice Lock Recheck
revision_instructions.instruction: Repair flagged 14-point audit failures, especially register, matter-of-fact marvels, emotion rendering, connective flow, narrator compliance, and concrete detail.
Recast Dorothy-facing narration and NPC phrasing into the diction fingerprint defined in Voice Lock, avoiding modern conceptual vocabulary.
Remove the non-canon 'go back to Oz first' end option and keep the close pointed directly toward westward departure.
Make the session close use the same commitment state and next-session bridge already established in Phase 5/6 for S2_C4.
Add subtle in-world acknowledgment lines for every state-changing choice path currently marked with no signal or only abstract summary.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The opening material before the cut is elegant but dramatically soft. Paragraph by paragraph, the answer to "Can a new user feel the stakes within 60 seconds of this moment?" stays NO through the Lion waking, the recap of the poppy rescue, the resumed road journey, the green countryside, the farmhouse meal, the villagers' lore about Oz, the gate procedure, the spectacle fitting, the city tour, the palace waiting, and Dorothy's luxurious room. All of that content builds atmosphere, promise, and curiosity, but not immediate playable tension. Even the audience-with-Oz setup remains delayed because the actual coercive problem has not yet landed on the page in the provided excerpt. The first clear YES arrives when Dorothy is finally brought toward the Throne Room to face Oz alone: at that point a new user can immediately feel vulnerability, uncertainty, and the possibility of a consequential demand. That is the first moment where the emotional question becomes active rather than merely anticipated: what will this immense authority ask of you, and what will it cost to get home? Everything before that is preamble for interactive purposes. It should be cut because it spends the retention window on travelogue, hospitality, visual description, and deferred exposition instead of live danger or decision pressure. The cold open should therefore begin with Dorothy being led to the Throne Room, compressing the Emerald City wonder into sensory details in motion while foregrounding the fear of meeting power alone.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Chapter XI, at the moment Dorothy is fetched after breakfast and taken toward the Throne Room to meet Oz alone; operationally this session should begin at Event 99, with prior setup folded into brief action-led context.
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 2 chapters of setup material in the provided pages, ending just before the Oz audience sequence; effectively all pre-audience lead-in before Event 99.
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Travel recap, recovery beat, countryside description, Emerald City arrival spectacle, lore exposition about Oz, hospitality scenes, palace lodging, and passive wonder-building.
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Dorothy is in Emerald City seeking a way home; each companion wants something from Oz; Oz only admits them one at a time; the city's enforced green spectacles create its dazzling look; Dorothy travels with Toto and three companions whose hopes depend on this meeting.

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
The green silk at your wrists whispers as you walk, and the locked spectacles press cold against your temples. You are Dorothy, and though your stomach tightens, you keep step behind the palace girl instead of turning back.

Courtiers crowd the hall in bright green layers, pretending not to stare. Toto squirms in your arms, then goes still, his ears pricked toward the tall doors ahead. No one here has seen Oz plainly. Yet you have crossed forests, hunger, and fear to ask one thing of him: send you home.

Your friends are not beside you now. The Scarecrow waits for brains, the Woodman for a heart, the Lion for courage—but this walk is yours alone. If Oz is kind, everything may change. If he is terrible, you may have brought them all this far for nothing.

The palace girl stops before the Throne Room doors, bows, and leaves you there as something vast stirs on the other side.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: confrontation. A user arrives feeling vulnerable and wanting to prove they can face power without losing themselves.

setup.source_moment: Chapter XI, after breakfast, when the green maiden fetches Dorothy and leads her through the court toward the Throne Room to meet Oz alone; operational cold open begins at Event 99 as established in Phase 3.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the first live moment of dramatic vulnerability. Dorothy is separated from her companions, approaching concentrated authority, and the question of what Oz will demand becomes immediate. It satisfies the cut-not-add law because all prior Emerald City arrival material can be folded into moving sensory context rather than played as preamble.

escalation.source_moment: Dorothy's audience with Oz as the Great Head, followed by the companions' separate audiences and the regrouping in which they compare what Oz demanded.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is clear: secure Dorothy's way home and the companions' hoped-for gifts. The obstacle is equally clear: Oz will grant nothing unless the party kills the Wicked Witch of the West. Stakes compound because the demand is coercive, the authority is intimidating, and the quest now requires action against a feared enemy rather than simple petition.

breath.source_moment: The companions compare Oz's wildly different forms and demands after their separate audiences: Dorothy saw a Great Head, others encounter alternate manifestations, creating absurdity and wonder in the mismatch between private revelations.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This provides the required release through comic absurdity and uncanny wonder. The same ruler appearing as different impossible forms to each traveler gives the player a reward beat between intimidation and westward commitment. It also keeps tension alive because the humor raises suspicion rather than dissolving stakes.

twist.source_moment: The party must decide whether to go west and attempt the Witch quest after learning Oz's bargain is the only offered path to Dorothy's return and their own desires.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is a moral-weight moment because Dorothy must choose between accepting an unjust bargain, defying it, or reframing it on her own terms. There is no correct answer: obedience may preserve hope, defiance preserves dignity, and reluctant compromise reflects survival under coercion. All options cost something.

resolution.source_moment: The company leaves Emerald City under the new burden of Oz's command, heading west toward the Wicked Witch's country.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The primary arc of this session resolves in one sense: the uncertainty of what Oz wants is answered, and the next quest path is defined. The seed for the next session is planted explicitly: the westward mission against the Witch. The session should end not on travel summary but on the player's own unresolved commitment about how Dorothy will undertake that mission.

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Identity - obedience_vs_defiance. How do you enter the Throne Room before power has spoken?
branching_choices.dramatic_question: When faced with Oz's unseen authority, do you present yourself meekly, respectfully but firmly, or with plainspoken resistance to being frightened?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Methodology - trust_vs_caution. How much faith do you place in Oz's spectacle and promise once he speaks?
branching_choices.dramatic_question: Do you accept Oz as a real path home, question the terms and truth of what he claims, or agree while openly reserving judgment?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Moral Weight - obedience_vs_defiance. Response to an unjust coercive bargain.
branching_choices.dramatic_question: Will you agree to go west because there is no other door open, submit fully to Oz's command, or declare that if you face the Witch it will be by your own choice and not his right?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Future Commitment - interdependence_vs_self_reliance. How will Dorothy undertake the westward mission with the company?
branching_choices.dramatic_question: As the road turns west, do you lean on the company as a shared undertaking, assign each companion a role in the danger ahead, or inwardly decide that getting home will finally depend on your own resolve?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Dorothy waiting outside the Throne Room doors with Toto in her arms before first seeing Oz.
Dorothy asks to be sent back to Kansas during her audience with Oz.
Regrouping with the companions after the separate audiences, especially as each reveals his private hope and fear.
Comparing the different forms Oz appeared in to each traveler.
The absurd mismatch in Oz's forms begins to feel less majestic and more troubling.

posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy meets fear by moving closer to truth rather than shrinking from it.

posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy notices theatrical details and tests whether Oz feels real.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy speaks gently even while afraid, preserving courtesy under pressure.

posture_shifts.placement: late Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy pushes harder when home is held behind conditions.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy copes with uncanniness through lightness and shared humor.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy avoids saying how shaken she is by talking around the strangeness.

posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: Dorothy attends to companions' hopes before declaring her own course.
