scene_integrity.emotional_context: Fresh disappointment after the balloon failure; hope is desperate, brittle, and immediately tested.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: The Monkeys refuse Kansas, one use is spent, and the party seeks local advice about Glinda.
fold_back_path.why: Canon requires the failed Monkey solution and the pivot toward the South road.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Pressing the command as if force of will should break the rule.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Requesting the truth within the rule of the Cap.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Testing for partial utility and workable limits.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: Hope collides with law, and Dorothy reveals whether she meets limits by trust, defiance, or calculation.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch opportunity: session 5, event 179, acceptance_of_limits_vs_insistence -> payoff session 6, events 208-210 with Glinda.

branching_choices.source_moment: On the southern road, the Fighting Trees seize the Scarecrow and bar the party's way into the forest.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The road to Glinda does not end politely; it lashes back. Branches stoop like arms, the Scarecrow goes flying, and the wood itself seems to have taken sides against strangers. You have only a breath to decide what kind of passage this will be.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you mean to get your company through the Fighting Trees?

options.label: A
options.text: You hold the others back and study the trees for a pattern before anyone commits to a path.
options.outcome: You do not rush a second body into the branches after the first. Instead you make the party wait while you watch the trees bend, strike, and settle again. The pause costs pride, but it buys understanding. The Scarecrow, shaken but unhurt, proves useful by being flung twice; the Lion prowls the edge, and the Tin Woodman marks which trunks can reach and which cannot. Soon the truth shows itself: only the first row fights like sentries, while the deeper wood stands still. That knowledge turns panic into method. When the Woodman finally advances with his axe, it is not blind violence but a chosen breach, and the others run through in the moment his stroke opens. The forest has not been conquered, only read correctly.
options.downstream_effect: The party crosses with a more coordinated plan, and the Scarecrow's usefulness in danger becomes more visible to everyone.

npc_dispositions.shift: Trust increases because Dorothy let observation and his resilience matter.
Respect increases because force was used only after thought.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Crossed the Fighting Trees by reading the danger before acting.
defining_moral_choices: Chose patience under threat rather than immediate force.

options.world_noticed_signal: After the first hacked branch, the deeper trees stand as still as carved things. The Scarecrow looks less like baggage and more like part of the answer.
options.defining_line: You stop long enough to learn what the danger actually is.

options.label: B
options.text: You urge the Tin Woodman to hack a way through at once and drive the whole party after him.
options.outcome: There is no time, in your mind, for the forest to teach lessons. You call for the axe and movement together, and the Tin Woodman answers with a stroke so fierce that the first branch falls cleanly away. The tree shudders as if in pain, but the opening is there, and you seize it. Everyone runs. The Lion shoulders through, Toto yelps as a twig catches him, and the Woodman chops him free without slowing. It is fast, loud, and a little wild, but it works before the wood can close itself again. On the farther side, breathless and safe, you leave behind a trembling line of branches. The passage feels less like solving a problem than breaking a spell by daring it to stop you twice.
options.downstream_effect: The party bursts through quickly, but the Tin Woodman and Toto visibly bear the strain of a more violent passage.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: Axe used heavily; recent_use marked FORCEFUL_CLEARING.

npc_dispositions.shift: Sorrow increases slightly at causing pain, though he remains loyal.
Confidence in Dorothy's decisiveness increases slightly.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed: Cut a living branch to force passage.
key_successes_failures: Forced a rapid crossing through the Fighting Trees.

options.world_noticed_signal: The severed branch writhes once upon the ground before going still. Toto keeps close to your skirts for several steps after he is freed.
options.defining_line: You make a road by refusing to wait for one.

options.label: C
options.text: You let each companion use what he does best and guide the crossing together.
options.outcome: You do not treat the danger as yours alone to master. The Scarecrow tests the reach because being tossed cannot truly harm him; the Lion finds the widest opening by scent and sight; the Tin Woodman takes the necessary stroke; and you keep Toto and the timing of the whole company in hand. The plan is simple because each part belongs to the right friend. When the branch bends, the axe answers. When the gap opens, all of you move at once, not in confusion but in order. Even Toto's brief capture is answered almost before fear can ripen, for the Woodman cuts him free and the company closes around him. By the time you reach the farther edge, the crossing feels like a proof of fellowship rather than mere escape.
options.downstream_effect: The party's shared competence becomes explicit, strengthening the sense that the southern road will be survived together or not at all.

npc_dispositions.shift: Belonging and confidence increase because his odd body is treated as useful.
Trust increases because Dorothy relied on his judgment without glorifying violence.
Loyalty increases because Dorothy made him part of the solution.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Continued south as a company rather than as Dorothy alone.
key_successes_failures: Cleared the Fighting Trees through coordinated companionship.

options.world_noticed_signal: When you come clear of the wood, the companions fall naturally back into step around you. The road south seems less lonely than it did a moment before.
options.defining_line: You cross by trusting each friend to be exactly himself.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The party exits the forest and reaches the high white china wall at the edge of the next country.

canon_boundaries: The Fighting Trees must block the southern road and physically seize intruders at the first row.
The Tin Woodman's axe can cut the attacking branch.
The party must succeed in crossing and continue south.
Only the first row behaves as active sentries.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: He will volunteer to test danger because being thrown does not injure him much and he wants to be useful.
He will use the axe when needed, but any needless cruelty toward living things troubles him.
He may fear the uncanny forest, yet he still offers protection and practical help.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: The party does not yet know only the first row of trees fights until they observe or test it; no one knows what lies beyond the forest wall.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Travel hope has turned into sudden physical threat; the road now demands method instead of mere willingness.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: The Tin Woodman cuts an attacking branch and the company passes through the first row into the forest interior.
fold_back_path.why: Canon requires a successful crossing that demonstrates the companions' usefulness on the South road.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Immediate hacking and rushing through before the danger can reset.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Assigning roles and crossing in coordinated order.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Observing first and exploiting the pattern once understood.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: The choice is not whether to continue, but what kind of problem-solver Dorothy becomes when the world attacks back.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Cross-session payoff plan: S3 event 112 interdependence_vs_self_reliance echoes in session 5 events 203-205; this choice reinforces that dimension toward session 6 farewell dynamics.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the Dainty China Country, the travelers frighten a china cow, the milkmaid's world is broken, and Dorothy is blamed for harm she did not intend.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The white floor shines under your feet like a plate, and everything upon it is bright, tiny, and terribly breakable. Then the cow kicks, the stool and pail go over, and the milkmaid cries, "See what you have done!" You have not meant to be cruel, but your very size has become a kind of danger.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you answer the harm your journey is causing in the China Country?

options.label: A
options.text: You keep the company moving and accept that fragile folk may suffer when greater needs press hard upon you.
options.outcome: The milkmaid's anger stings, but you do not let it root you to the spot. You offer sorrow enough to be honest, then gather the company onward before apology can become delay. Kansas, Glinda, the desert, the spent command of the Cap—these weigh more heavily in you than a country where even a startled step may ruin something. The china people shrink from your passing, and their tiny reproaches follow like chips of broken glaze. The Tin Woodman goes quieter, for he cannot like such injury, while the Lion keeps close as if to shield you from blame as much as danger. You do not call the harm nothing. You only decide that home, and the road toward it, must remain the larger claim, however uneasy that feels beneath your feet.
options.downstream_effect: The party crosses faster, but the China Country's inhabitants react with sharper fear and resentment, and the moral bruise follows into later scenes.

npc_dispositions.shift: Moral unease increases because Dorothy prioritized urgency over repair.
Protective solidarity increases as the world turns accusatory.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Frightened and indirectly damaged china lives while pressing onward.
defining_moral_choices: Chose necessity over gentleness in a fragile world.
crimes_committed_or_witnessed: Accepted collateral breakage while continuing south.

options.world_noticed_signal: Tiny shutters close as you pass, and bright little faces vanish from painted windows. The country does not stop you, but it does not trust your tread.
options.defining_line: You decide that sorrow does not always grant the right to stop.

options.label: B
options.text: You apologize, move as lightly as you can, and continue only with the care your quest can honestly afford.
options.outcome: You do not deny the damage, and you do not pretend you can undo it. Instead you bow your head to the milkmaid, speak your regret plainly, and then set the company in motion with stricter care. The Lion shortens his stride; Toto is hushed against further barking; the Scarecrow warns of what lies near your feet; and the Tin Woodman picks the cleanest line through the little streets. It is slower, but not ruinously so. The china people remain offended, yet some watch from doorways rather than fleeing altogether, as if they can see you are trying to be less dangerous than your size permits. The road south continues under a new burden: not guilt alone, but the knowledge that good intentions must be translated into smaller steps.
options.downstream_effect: The party still advances, but their movement through the China Country becomes visibly more careful and measured.

npc_dispositions.shift: Approval increases because Dorothy paired necessity with conscience.
Trust increases because Dorothy turned remorse into practical adjustment.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Acknowledged harm to china folk and adjusted conduct.
defining_moral_choices: Balanced apology with the need to continue south.
promises_kept_or_broken: Tried to move gently after causing damage.

options.world_noticed_signal: After your apology, the next few little figures do not run at once, though they keep well back from your shoes. Even the china road seems to ring less sharply beneath your steps.
options.defining_line: You carry guilt forward and let it teach your feet.

options.label: C
options.text: You slow the whole crossing and put the safety of the china people above your speed to Glinda.
options.outcome: The milkmaid's broken little world is enough to stop you. You kneel as far as you safely can, apologize with all your heart, and then command the crossing to change. Toto is held close, the Lion waits for your word before each stride, and every footfall is chosen as if it might matter to a kingdom. The journey grows slower at once, perhaps dangerously slower, yet the country itself seems to breathe easier around you. Some of the china folk still glare, but others peep from behind painted fences rather than vanishing in terror. The Tin Woodman's face softens, and even the Scarecrow becomes careful in his flopping, awkward way. You cannot mend the cow's leg or the milkmaid's elbow, but you can refuse to add thoughtless injury to what has already been done.
options.downstream_effect: Progress slows, but the company's conduct visibly changes, and the China Country responds with less panic and more wary tolerance.

npc_dispositions.shift: Deep approval increases because Dorothy chose gentleness over haste.
Affection increases because Dorothy values vulnerable lives even under pressure.
Future regard improves indirectly because Dorothy's conduct in fragile lands becomes part of her moral pattern.

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy: Prioritized fragile lives over speed.
npcs_helped_or_harmed: Minimized further harm in the China Country after accidental damage.
defining_moral_choices: Chose care over urgency in a morally costly crossing.

options.world_noticed_signal: The tiny barking dog falls silent first, as if the country itself has sensed your altered tread. Painted doors remain open a crack behind you instead of slamming shut.
options.defining_line: You let the smallest lives set the pace.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The party continues south through and beyond the China Country, carrying the ethical consequence of the crossing into the session's resolution.

canon_boundaries: China Country is physically fragile, and careless movement must create breakage and social consequence.
The milkmaid's accusation and visible damage remain true.
Dorothy cannot fully repair the broken china herself in this scene.
The party must still continue south through the country.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: He is troubled by harm to delicate beings and responds warmly to visible care, but he will continue if Dorothy insists.
He tries to help practically and may become awkwardly careful once the fragility is understood.
She remains angry and protective of her damaged world; she may accept apology in tone only faintly, not instantly become friendly.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: The party understands the country is fragile but does not yet know the full social response of all its inhabitants; the china folk know only that giants have frightened and damaged them.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Wonder has turned into moral discomfort; Dorothy's need for home now conflicts with responsibility toward those her quest endangers.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: Dorothy acknowledges the incident in some form and the company continues south with a changed tone of movement.
fold_back_path.why: Canon requires the milkmaid breakage to reveal the cost of passing through fragile worlds, but the journey cannot end here.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Pressing onward despite harm because the larger need feels urgent.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Submitting the crossing to strict care even at meaningful delay.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Accepting harm, apologizing, and adjusting without surrendering the route.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: This is the session's true moral bruise: how much room Dorothy makes for the vulnerable when her own longing is righteous.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch opportunity: session 5, event 191, care_vs_curiosity -> payoff session 6, events 207-212 in Glinda's reading of Dorothy's conduct through fragile worlds.

branching_choices.source_moment: After leaving the China Country behind, Dorothy takes stock of the dangerous southern road and decides what promise to make about Glinda, home, and the companions who still walk with her.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The road south has ceased to be a hope spoken in a palace and become a real thing under your shoes. One command of the Cap is gone, strange countries lie behind and before you, and your friends are still with you by choice. Night seems near enough to confess what you mean to ask of them next.
branching_choices.choice_question: What promise do you make about the road to Glinda?

options.label: A
options.text: You promise to go on only so long as each friend freely chooses the road beside you.
options.outcome: You look from one dear face to another and refuse to make your homecoming into a silent command. If they go on, it must be because they choose it afresh, danger by danger. The promise steadies the company rather than loosening it. The Lion lifts his head, the Scarecrow answers at once that he is not leaving, and the Tin Woodman seems moved by the fairness of being asked rather than assumed. Yet the road ahead is not softened. By speaking so, you admit that companionship is not possession and that love may require release before the journey is done. The comfort this gives is real, but so is the fear hidden inside it: one day a friend may choose a different duty, and you will have promised to let him.
options.downstream_effect: The next session opens with the companions' agency foregrounded, making future departures feel earned rather than forced.

npc_dispositions.shift: Trust increases because Dorothy honors his freedom as well as his loyalty.
Affection increases because Dorothy does not take his devotion for granted.
Respect increases because Dorothy frames fellowship as chosen duty.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Will continue only with companions who freely choose the road.
defining_moral_choices: Put consent and fellowship alongside the quest for home.

options.world_noticed_signal: The company draws closer, not because you have bound them, but because you have not. Even the darkening road seems to make room for such an honest promise.
options.defining_line: You love them enough to leave them free.
options.next_session_opens: At dawn the southern road leads into a land where another creature's need will test whether chosen loyalty can survive separation.

options.label: B
options.text: You promise to keep going toward Glinda, but not at the cost of ignoring what the road demands of each companion.
options.outcome: Your promise is neither hard as iron nor soft as tears. You mean to reach Glinda; that remains the star by which you walk. But you also say aloud that the road may ask different things of different hearts, and when it does, you will not pretend otherwise. The words make room for compromise, for pause, for choices not yet visible. The Scarecrow nods thoughtfully, as if he hears in them a kind of practical wisdom. The Tin Woodman accepts them gravely. The Lion seems relieved that courage need not always mean the same act in every breast. Nothing is settled by this except your honesty: you will keep moving homeward, but you will not call every sacrifice identical merely because it serves your wish.
options.downstream_effect: The next session inherits a flexible but emotionally charged group philosophy, ready to bend around sacrifice rather than deny it.

npc_dispositions.shift: Respect increases for Dorothy's balanced judgment.
Trust increases because Dorothy acknowledges moral complexity.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Will continue toward Glinda while honoring each companion's differing duty.
defining_moral_choices: Refused both selfish haste and total self-denial.

options.world_noticed_signal: No one speaks for a little while after that, but the silence is thoughtful rather than hurt. The road ahead feels uncertain in shape, yet no longer dishonest.
options.defining_line: You promise the road, not the exact form of its cost.
options.next_session_opens: Morning finds the company still together, but the first cry for help from the wild country will demand a promise more specific than this one.

options.label: C
options.text: You promise that you will press on to Glinda unless your friends themselves can no longer keep up with your need to get home.
options.outcome: You have been delayed, deceived, and turned back too often to dress your need in gentler cloth. So you say what is plain in you: you must reach Glinda, and you mean to press on. Your friends may stay with you if they can, and you love them for it, but your homeward longing will not wait upon every doubt the road invents. The words are brave, a little fierce, and not entirely easy to hear. The Lion's ears flick back before he squares himself again. The Tin Woodman grows solemn. The Scarecrow, loyal as ever, answers with immediate resolve. The promise gives the journey force, but it also plants a question that will not be settled tonight—whether love can keep pace when one heart is determined to outrun grief.
options.downstream_effect: The next session opens under stronger personal urgency, making any companion divergence feel sharper and more emotionally exposed.

npc_dispositions.shift: Protective loyalty increases because Dorothy's need is spoken plainly.
Concern increases because Dorothy has set home above shared pace.
Anxious devotion increases; he wants to keep up with Dorothy's urgency.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Will press toward Glinda with home as the overriding claim.
defining_moral_choices: Prioritized personal destiny over equal pacing with companions.

options.world_noticed_signal: The night air seems thinner after you say it, as if the road itself has straightened. Your friends do not leave you, but each carries the promise in a different silence.
options.defining_line: You stop apologizing for wanting home more than anything.
options.next_session_opens: The next dawn breaks on a wild summons from the forest, and your vow to press on is challenged by a crown that is not yours.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The session closes on Dorothy's declared philosophy of the road, carrying the company into the next southern hazard without resolving which companionly cost will come due first.

canon_boundaries: Dorothy must remain committed to seeking Glinda.
The companions are still present and willing at session end.
No final farewell or separation resolves within this session.
The choice must seed next-session sacrifice and the Lion's destiny without prematurely enacting it.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: He may fear what lies ahead, but if Dorothy speaks from the heart he answers with loyalty and a wish to protect her.
He is quick to reaffirm his devotion to Dorothy and slow to place his own comfort first.
He listens gravely and measures promises by their kindness as well as their practicality.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: No one yet knows the exact next hazard or the Lion's coming kingship; all only understand that stranger and costlier trials lie ahead before Glinda.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: The session's despair has become a viable quest, but wonder and moral bruise have matured Dorothy into someone who must now define what companionship means under pressure.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: Dorothy commits to continue south with the company under a stated philosophy about companions and home.
fold_back_path.why: The session must end on Dorothy's unresolved decision rather than on a plot event resolving itself.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Prioritizing the urgent homeward vow even if companions must struggle to match it.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Binding the road to freely renewed consent and fair companionship.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Committing to the goal while leaving room for the road's changing demands.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: The player is not choosing what happens next, but what promise Dorothy will have to live up to when next happens.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch opportunity: session 5, event 199, duty_to_companions_vs_personal_destiny -> payoff session 6, events 209-212 in Lion farewell and companion release.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Immediately after the Monkey King refuses Kansas and departs, leaving Dorothy with a wasted use of the Golden Cap.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The window is empty again. The Cap has obeyed you, and still you are no nearer home.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you let the hurt show?

options.label: A
options.text: You swallow the tears and ask at once what can be done next.
options.outcome: Your throat aches with the effort of being steady, but you make yourself useful before sorrow can take the room. The Scarecrow answers that steadiness by thinking harder, and even the Soldier, when summoned, seems to regard you less as a child to be comforted than as a traveler to be advised. The pain does not lessen; it merely puts on its shoes and walks beside you. Your companions feel the loss, yet they also take courage from the way you do not let it master your tongue.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows brisker and more composed, emphasizing resilience through action.

npc_dispositions.shift: Respect increases slightly for Dorothy's composure.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Met disappointment with immediate resolve.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Scarecrow stops fussing over your face and begins at once to fuss over the problem instead.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the disappointment break through and speak your grief plainly.
options.outcome: The words come out with the tears close behind them, and there is relief in not pretending otherwise. The Tin Woodman answers with such tender sorrow that the room softens around you instead of hardening. Your grief names the cost of the lost command in a way no one can ignore, and because it is spoken openly, your companions gather nearer rather than stepping back. You are not less brave for hurting where others can see it; only more plainly human in a palace full of strange things.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens into exposed feeling and shared consolation.

npc_dispositions.shift: Tenderness increases because Dorothy did not hide her hurt.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Spoke disappointment honestly instead of disguising it.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Tin Woodman leans nearer at once, as if sorrow spoken aloud were something he understands especially well.

options.label: C
options.text: You admit the loss, but turn it over aloud as a mistake to be learned from.
options.outcome: You do not deny the sting, yet neither do you sit down under it. Instead you name the wasted command for what it is and begin, even while hurting, to sort what the failure has taught you. The Scarecrow brightens at that, for thought is the nearest thing he knows to comfort. Your companions hear in your voice both sadness and usefulness, and the mood of the room shifts from pity toward planning. Home is still far away, but error has at least become knowledge, and knowledge is a kind of lantern.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes reflective and practical, turning sorrow into insight.

npc_dispositions.shift: Admiration increases for Dorothy's practical resilience.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: Treated magical refusal as useful knowledge.

options.world_noticed_signal: The room's hush changes character; it is no longer the silence after a blow, but the silence before an idea.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Soldier with the Green Whiskers is summoned and Glinda is named.

emotional_choices.source_moment: When the Scarecrow, Lion, and Tin Woodman each volunteer to accompany Dorothy south.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: One by one they offer themselves to the road as if your journey were already partly theirs. Their loyalty is so ready that it almost hurts.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you receive their fellowship?

options.label: A
options.text: You thank them warmly and confess that you do not know how you would go without them.
options.outcome: The words come with simple truth in them, and that truth binds more surely than ceremony. The Lion stands taller for being needed; the Scarecrow beams; and the Tin Woodman seems touched that usefulness can be so dearly spoken of. By admitting your dependence, you do not become smaller in their eyes. You become more plainly the center of a company that has chosen one another as much as any destination. The road south begins not in loneliness but in shared purpose.
options.tonal_effect: Narration warms into ensemble tenderness and mutual need.

npc_dispositions.shift: Confidence increases because Dorothy openly needs him.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Accepted the company as true companions in the southern quest.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Lion's tail gives a pleased sweep, and even the Scarecrow seems to stand more firmly on his stuffed legs.

options.label: B
options.text: You thank them gravely and promise not to forget what they are risking for you.
options.outcome: You receive their loyalty as something weighty, not casual, and your gratitude gives it dignity. The Tin Woodman answers that with grave kindness of his own, while the Scarecrow seems proud to have his devotion taken seriously. The company forms around a sense of chosen duty rather than mere cheer. There is comfort in that, though also a quiet ache, because every kindness now carries the shadow of cost. You begin the road south already mindful that love is asking something real of those who walk beside you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes solemn, loyal, and duty-shaped.

npc_dispositions.shift: Respect increases because Dorothy honors sacrifice.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Marked the southern journey as a shared duty.

options.world_noticed_signal: Their answering looks are quieter after that, but deeper, as if a jesting promise has become a real vow.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell them they ought not to trouble themselves so much for your sake, even while you are glad of them.
options.outcome: Your protest is half modesty and half fear of burdening what you love. It makes the Lion insist more stoutly on protecting you, and the Scarecrow answer with cheerful stubbornness. The Tin Woodman, tender as ever, seems to understand that your demurring is another form of affection. The company does not weaken; if anything, it presses nearer around the very reluctance with which you accept it. Yet a thread of guilt now runs alongside your gratitude, and it will color how you ask things of them later.
options.tonal_effect: Narration gains a note of guilty affection and protective push-pull.

npc_dispositions.shift: Protectiveness increases in response to Dorothy's reluctance.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Accepted help while resisting being a burden.

options.world_noticed_signal: Your friends answer your hesitation by closing ranks around it, as if your doubt itself were something to guard.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The company departs Emerald City together.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The party takes a last look back at Emerald City and reflects on Oz's mixed legacy.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The towers and green walls lie behind you now, beautiful and a little false. Oz has failed you, yet not altogether without kindness.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling do you carry away from the city?

options.label: A
options.text: You think chiefly of the promise he broke and let the hurt remain sharp.
options.outcome: You do not smooth the truth into something prettier than it was. Oz promised home and did not deliver it; that fact pricks at every bright tower behind you. The city remains lovely, but it no longer has the power to enchant you quite so fully. Your companions do not rebuke the feeling, for each of them has his own bargain with the old humbug. The road ahead seems cleaner for your refusal to be charmed by failure dressed in green silk.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows clearer and less sentimental about authority.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: Carried the truth of Oz's failure forward without softening it.

options.world_noticed_signal: The city's brightness looks more like paint from this distance than magic.

options.label: B
options.text: You forgive him as a good man who could not do all he promised.
options.outcome: The hurt remains, but it is no longer the only truth you carry. Oz failed you, yet he also tried in the poor, human way he knew. That thought leaves your heart less burdened than anger would have done, and the city behind you keeps some of its beauty without keeping your trust. Forgiveness does not mend the road home, but it clears a little bitterness from the path beneath your feet.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes generous, tempered, and quietly sad.

npc_dispositions.shift: Future warmth increases indirectly because Dorothy leaves Emerald City graciously.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Forgave failed authority without forgetting the failure.

options.world_noticed_signal: The green domes seem softer as they sink behind the fields, as if distance has taken some sting from them.

options.label: C
options.text: You hold both truths together and leave with gratitude for what was given and clarity about what was not.
options.outcome: You do not choose between gratitude and judgment, because both belong to the truth. Oz gave what he could to your friends and failed where your deepest need began. By carrying both facts together, you leave the city neither embittered nor bewitched. The companions' talk settles around that same balance, and the journey south feels more mature for it. You have learned that goodness and insufficiency can live in the same person, which is a heavy lesson for so small a traveler.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes balanced, morally clear, and quietly grown.

npc_dispositions.shift: Respect increases because Dorothy honors complexity.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Held gratitude and disappointment together without denying either.

options.world_noticed_signal: Behind you the city remains beautiful, but no longer simple.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The first day's travel and night pass; by morning the party reaches the thick wood.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The first view over the wall into the Dainty China Country.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: You lift your head above the white wall and the whole queer little country shines below like a toy kingdom set upon a platter. "Oh, my!" escapes before thought can catch it.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What kind of wonder takes hold of you first?

options.label: A
options.text: You stare in delighted curiosity, trying to take in every tiny detail at once.
options.outcome: Your eyes dart from painted roofs to china sheep to jeweled little princes, greedy in the innocent way wonder makes one greedy. The country below seems not merely pretty but inexhaustible, as if every inch of it might hold some new bright absurdity. Your delight spreads to the others, and even the dangers of the southern road loosen their grip for a moment. The world has become strange again in a rewarding way, and you let yourself be fed by it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration brightens into quick, delighted visual abundance.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Received wonder as nourishment on the road.

options.world_noticed_signal: Even Toto's tiny bark seems part of the scene's merriment rather than a threat.

options.label: B
options.text: You laugh outright at the absurd prettiness of it all.
options.outcome: The laugh comes like a spring after strain, and it does you good. There is something so earnest and so ridiculous in a whole country of china clowns and shepherdesses that mirth feels like the truest courtesy to it. The others catch the mood at once. For a little while the road south is not a burden but an adventure fit to astonish anybody. The laughter does not mock the place; it celebrates the sheer boldness of such a world existing at all.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns playful, airy, and lightly comic.

npc_dispositions.shift: Ease increases because Dorothy lets delight break tension.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Found joy and release amid danger.

options.world_noticed_signal: The companions' exclamations come easier after your laughter, and the wall-top feels less like a lookout than a holiday perch.

options.label: C
options.text: You fall quiet before it, as if the little country were too delicate for loud delight.
options.outcome: Wonder comes over you not as a shout but as a hush. The tiny houses, the bright little figures, the whole polished spread of the country seems so neat and delicate that your very pleasure grows careful. The others still cry "Oh, my!" in their several ways, but your own amazement settles into reverence. It is the kind of beauty that asks one not only to look, but to mind oneself while looking. Without knowing it yet, you are already preparing your heart for gentleness.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes hushed, precise, and tenderly observant.

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy: Entered a fragile wonder with instinctive restraint.

options.world_noticed_signal: Toto's bark seems suddenly too sharp for the white bright stillness below, and your hand moves to quiet him almost before you think.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The party prepares to descend into the China Country.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Dorothy hushes Toto and chooses how to regard the tiny china people before entering their country.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The little purple dog runs barking below, and all at once the country seems less like a toy and more like somebody's home. Your next movement may already be too large.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you prepare yourself to enter among them?

options.label: A
options.text: You hush Toto at once and resolve to tread as though every step were near a cradle.
options.outcome: You gather Toto close and let caution settle over your delight without spoiling it. The china country becomes, in your mind, not merely curious but entrusted to your care while you pass through it. The companions notice and answer in kind; even the Lion seems to place his paws more thoughtfully. This gentleness does not yet know what it will cost, but it gives the crossing a moral shape before the first accident ever happens.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes protective, delicate, and morally attentive.

npc_dispositions.shift: Warmth increases because Dorothy instinctively protects the small and fragile.

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy: Chose careful entry into a vulnerable place.

options.world_noticed_signal: Toto settles under your hand, and the place below seems to wait rather than flinch.

options.label: B
options.text: You quiet Toto, but keep your eyes bright for whatever strange things the little country may show you.
options.outcome: You do not forget caution, yet curiosity keeps hold of you too. The china folk are fragile, certainly, but they are also marvelous, and you mean to see them as more than breakable ornaments. So you hush Toto and prepare to move carefully while still looking closely. The balance gives the scene a lively tenderness: wonder with its sleeves rolled up. It is a good way to enter a strange place, though not a foolproof one.
options.tonal_effect: Narration balances fascination with practical restraint.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Paired curiosity with caution in a fragile world.

options.world_noticed_signal: Your gaze keeps moving even as your hand stills Toto, and the country below seems both inviting and precarious.

options.label: C
options.text: You hush Toto, but mostly because you do not mean to let a tiny barking dog delay the road.
options.outcome: Your care is real, but it is impatient care. Toto must be quiet because the road is already long and this pretty country is still something to get across. The china people strike you first as an obstacle made charming, and only second as neighbors in their own right. Nothing cruel lies in that, only urgency. Yet urgency has a way of making even gentle hands a little rougher than they intended to be.
options.tonal_effect: Narration keeps a restless undercurrent beneath the beauty.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Framed a delicate world first as a passage to be made.

options.world_noticed_signal: Toto falls quiet, but your weight is already angled toward the far side rather than the wonder at your feet.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The party descends, pats the Scarecrow back into shape, and begins crossing the China Country.

posture_shifts.placement: Cold open in the Throne Room as Dorothy lifts the Golden Cap, exposing hopeful urgency under pressure.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Your fingers close around the Golden Cap before doubt can sit down beside you.

options.label: A
options.text: You clutch it like the last good chance.
options.narration_adjustment: The gold seems warm from your own wanting. Everyone in the room feels, at least for a breath, that home may be only one command away.

options.label: B
options.text: You steady it first and breathe once.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not let hope make your hands foolish. The pause is small, but it gives the moment a kind of dignity.

options.label: C
options.text: You glance at your friends before speaking.
options.narration_adjustment: Their faces meet yours like a second courage. The wish for Kansas no longer feels entirely solitary.

posture_shifts.placement: After the Monkey King refuses Kansas, exposing whether Dorothy collapses inward or stays outwardly composed.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The empty window leaves the refusal hanging in the air like a thing with weight.

options.label: A
options.text: You look down before anyone can see your face.
options.narration_adjustment: For a moment the floor is easier to bear than their kindness. Grief folds close around you, small and private and sharp.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep your chin up and face it.
options.narration_adjustment: The hurt does not vanish, but it stands in plain sight with you. Your courage here is not triumph, only refusal to hide.

posture_shifts.placement: When the companions volunteer to travel south, exposing how warmly Dorothy receives their loyalty.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Their offers gather around you so quickly that gratitude and guilt arrive almost together.

options.label: A
options.text: You move toward them at once.
options.narration_adjustment: The company closes like a circle around a fire. It becomes plain that being loved is one of the road's provisions.

options.label: B
options.text: You smile, but keep a little distance.
options.narration_adjustment: Your thanks are real, though careful. Love is easier to bear when it does not stand too close to the fear of losing it.

options.label: C
options.text: You fuss over whether they should come at all.
options.narration_adjustment: Your first instinct is not to be helped, but not to cost others too much. The tenderness of that lands on them as clearly as any embrace.

posture_shifts.placement: On departure from Emerald City and the look back southward, exposing whether Dorothy reads the road as adventure or burden.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Once the green walls are behind you, the southern road seems to lengthen merely because you are looking at it.

options.label: A
options.text: You square yourself to it like a challenge.
options.narration_adjustment: Distance becomes something to meet rather than fear. The fields ahead look wide instead of empty.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel its weight before its promise.
options.narration_adjustment: The road is no ribbon from a storybook now, but a true stretch of uncertainty. Even hope has a tiredness in it.

posture_shifts.placement: At the first attack of the Fighting Trees, exposing whether Dorothy reacts with alarm, analysis, or brisk focus.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The Scarecrow flies backward through the air so suddenly that thought and fear arrive together.

options.label: A
options.text: You dart to him first.
options.narration_adjustment: Your body chooses care before strategy. It is a quick, loving movement that reminds the others what kind of leader you are.

options.label: B
options.text: You stare at the branches to understand them.
options.narration_adjustment: Even surprise cannot keep your mind from searching for a pattern. The forest becomes a puzzle a heartbeat before it becomes an enemy.

options.label: C
options.text: You call everyone into order at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Your voice lands before panic can spread. The danger still feels strange, but less ownerless.

posture_shifts.placement: At the wall reveal of the China Country, exposing capacity for delight and lightness.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The sight below is so pretty and queer that your heart almost forgets to be tired.

options.label: A
options.text: You grin before you can help it.
options.narration_adjustment: Wonder takes you by surprise and improves you at once. The road rewards you for having stayed upon it this far.

options.label: B
options.text: You let out a little laugh.
options.narration_adjustment: The laugh slips through the cracks left by strain. For an instant, Oz feels less like a trial than a marvelous absurdity.

posture_shifts.placement: Before descending into the China Country, exposing curiosity toward the tiny world and its inhabitants.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: From the wall-top, every painted roof and miniature fence seems to ask whether you mean to notice it properly.

options.label: A
options.text: You lean forward to study the little people.
options.narration_adjustment: The country becomes more than scenery at once. It gathers individuality under your gaze—homes, habits, and lives.

options.label: B
options.text: You look first for the safest way down.
options.narration_adjustment: Beauty does not distract you from consequence. Even wonder must be entered somehow, and you mean to do it without harm if you can.

options.label: C
options.text: You glance already toward the far side.
options.narration_adjustment: The prettiness cannot wholly detain your longing. Somewhere beyond this delicate brightness, Glinda still waits.

posture_shifts.placement: Immediately after the milkmaid's accusation, exposing Dorothy's first pulse of empathy, shame, or defensiveness.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The broken china gleams at your feet with a brightness that makes the damage harder to bear.

options.label: A
options.text: You flinch as if the break were in you.
options.narration_adjustment: The accident lands fully before any excuse can form. Your sorrow is quick, unguarded, and clean.

options.label: B
options.text: You stiffen under the blame.
options.narration_adjustment: The accusation feels larger than the act, and some part of you resists being made a villain by mere size. Compassion and self-protection jostle uneasily together.

options.label: C
options.text: You look at what can still be spared.
options.narration_adjustment: You feel the harm, but your mind goes at once to preventing the next one. Grief becomes carefulness in the very same breath.

posture_shifts.placement: Late in the session after emerging from the China Country, exposing whether Dorothy ends in resolve, caution, or fatigue.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: When the bright fragility lies behind you, the ordinary road looks almost stern in its plainness.

options.label: A
options.text: You set your face south again.
options.narration_adjustment: The day's lessons do not lighten the journey, but they do harden your willingness to continue it. Home remains far, and still you go.

options.label: B
options.text: You move on more carefully than before.
options.narration_adjustment: The road has taught you that harm can be done by the well-meaning as well as the cruel. Your hope now walks with better manners.

options.label: C
options.text: You let the tiredness show a little.
options.narration_adjustment: Courage is not less true for being weary. The road ahead does not seem impossible, only expensive in the coin of the heart.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Loyal, newly enthroned, eager to help Dorothy.
Tender-hearted and sorrowful for Dorothy's disappointment.
Protective and ready to accompany Dorothy.
Formal, bound, and respectful within strict magical limits.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: No one knows the Silver Shoes can solve Dorothy's problem; the Monkey King knows only the limits of the Monkeys' service; the companions do not yet know the route south until the Soldier is consulted.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Fresh after the balloon failure; resilience is promised but not yet proven.
canon_boundaries_active: Winged Monkeys cannot cross the desert to Kansas.
Golden Cap use must be spent on a valid summons.
Monkey King cannot violate geographic magical law.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to order the Monkeys to break canon travel limits.
Player asks for Silver Shoes solution prematurely.
Player tries to leave the Throne Room immediately without hearing the refusal.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Courtly, timid in the palace, helpful within what he knows.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The Soldier knows of Glinda and the dangerous southern road but not a guaranteed way across the desert; no one knows the specific hazards beyond rumor.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Disappointment is being converted into a viable new plan through advice and fellowship.
canon_boundaries_active: Glinda may know a way across the desert, but certainty is unavailable yet.
Dorothy's only actionable home path must shift to GLINDA_PLAN.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to summon Glinda directly.
Player demands impossible certainty from the Soldier.
Player attempts to remain permanently in Emerald City and end the quest.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Kindly procedural, surprised they would leave safety.
Committed to accompanying Dorothy despite his rulership.
Relieved to be out in the country again.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The party knows only that the road is straight south and full of dangers; they do not know the order or nature of those dangers yet.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Departure mixes gratitude, relief, and the ache of leaving behind a failed promise.
canon_boundaries_active: Green spectacles are removed at the gate and boxed.
The road south is the only current viable quest spine.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to remain in Emerald City after farewell beat.
Player attempts to use city authority or resources to bypass the southern route entirely.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Cheerfully brave about being the least breakable test case.
Ready to defend the party, uneasy about harming living things.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The party does not know at first that only the first row of trees is actively hostile; they have no knowledge yet of the wall beyond the forest.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: The journey's first southern danger arrives physically and without warning.
canon_boundaries_active: The Fighting Trees must seize and throw the Scarecrow.
The Tin Woodman's axe can open passage.
The party must make it through the forest.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to set the forest on fire or destroy the whole wood.
Player attempts to send Toto alone through the trees.
Player refuses to continue south after the attack.

available_objects: ladder made by the Tin Woodman

present_npcs.current_disposition: Curious, awkward, and game for absurd solutions.
Practical and quietly proud of his ladder.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: No one knows what lies beyond the wall until they look; the party does not yet understand how fragile the China Country will be underfoot.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the Throne Room, the Monkey King answers in a lowered, sorrowful voice and bows a second time before saying, "That cannot be done... we cannot cross the desert," making the refusal feel like a law rather than a rebuke.
paths.current_session_echo: Soldier with the Green Whiskers counsel scene: when summoned, he steps closer without ceremony and says, "Then we must ask who can help within the bounds of Oz," moving directly to Glinda instead of pausing to soothe a quarrel.
paths.next_session_payoff: Glinda audience opening exchange: when Dorothy asks for Kansas, Glinda studies her face and says, "You have learned to ask what magic may truly do before asking it to do more," then explains the Shoes without corrective sternness.
paths.later_session_legacy: At S6_C2, Glinda receives the Golden Cap with warmer trust; if Dorothy gives it promptly, Glinda's line becomes, "You have not clutched at power when truth was better."
paths.defining_line_captured: You ask for the truth before you ask for comfort.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The Monkey King's manner becomes ceremonially respectful instead of merely obedient, and the group's pivot to practical planning happens without emotional spillover.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Visible through the Monkey King's second bow, softened dialogue cadence, and the Soldier's brisk, matter-of-fact transition to naming Glinda.

paths.next_session_payoff: Glinda audience opening exchange: when Dorothy presses for Kansas, Glinda's first response is, "The South is not moved by urgency alone," and her explanation of the Shoes carries a corrective note before it turns kind.

consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Visible through the Monkey King's withheld second bow, the audible wing-rustle, the Soldier's pointed wording, and the Tin Woodman's stricken silence.

reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda explicitly reads Dorothy's manner through the Monkey scene: warm approval if Dorothy asked truthfully or tested limits well, corrective formality if Dorothy tried to command past the desert's law.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let Glinda's first line feel as though she has weighed Dorothy's whole road in a glance, naming the lesson without sounding like a scorekeeper.
