The session must end before the physical transport resolves.
No option may cancel departure or keep Dorothy in Oz.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: She honors Dorothy's inward choice and does not press sentimentally; her response is blessing, not interference.
He can bear sorrow if Dorothy is sincere, but he is deeply moved by promises of remembrance.
He responds through tears, gratitude, and concern that love not be denied in the name of composure.
He most strongly understands vows rooted in love and loyalty, and least enjoys brisk emotional severance.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Everyone present knows Dorothy can now go home by the Shoes; only Dorothy can decide the inward meaning of the step.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: The practical problem is solved; the unresolved question is what Dorothy chooses to carry into the solved ending.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: Dorothy forms an inward vow and prepares to click her heels.
fold_back_path.why: The retention mechanism here is her own unresolved decision, not a plot obstacle.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Vowing loyalty to love over convenience, even at the cost of safety or neat closure.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Vowing full return, duty, and wholehearted belonging to home.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Vowing memory and continuity between Oz and Kansas without denying either.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: What promise makes leaving bearable without undoing the truth of home?

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: S4:E173 Dorothy searches for Toto as balloon tugs -> loyalty_vs_escape becomes the emotional thesis of the ending.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Dorothy has just handed the Golden Cap to Glinda and feels the loss of the last thing in Oz that obeyed her.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The Cap is gone from your hands now. Relief comes with it, but so does the queer emptiness of having nothing left to command.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you feel the loss as Glinda turns from you to the others?

options.label: A
options.text: You let the relief be plain and breathe easier without the Cap.
options.outcome: You had not known how tightly you were holding yourself until the gold left your fingers. Without it, you feel smaller perhaps, but cleaner too, as if some harsh business has ended and you may be only a girl again. The relief shows in your face before you can hide it. Glinda notices, and so does the Tin Woodman, whose own spirit has always leaned toward gentle uses of strength. The moment does not stop the sadness of parting, but it places a little grace inside it: some burdens are mourned only until they are set down.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens toward release and moral lightness.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: felt relief in surrendering power

options.world_noticed_signal: Glinda's smile lingers a little, as if she has seen a hand unclench at the proper hour.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel suddenly bare and hate how quickly power can leave a person.
options.outcome: The emptiness comes sharp instead of peaceful. One moment the Cap was yours to call with, and the next you are only waiting upon another person's knowledge, another person's kindness. The feeling is not noble, but it is honest, and it makes the ruby chamber seem larger and less friendly than it did before. You stand your ground all the same. The hurt does not turn you back; it only reminds you how frightening dependence can be when you are far from Kansas and nearly at the end of all your strength.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows plainer, more exposed, and slightly raw.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: felt the sting of losing command

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You steady yourself by thinking the Cap may do more good in wiser hands.
options.outcome: You do not pretend the loss is easy, but you make sense of it as best you can. The Cap served you when you needed it; now it has passed to someone who understands its reach more fully than you ever could. That thought does not make you glad exactly, yet it keeps the surrender from feeling like waste. You watch Glinda with a more careful peace, measuring what follows not by your own possession of power but by whether power is used well. It is a grown thought, and it quiets you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration shifts toward composed reflection and practical moral balance.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: accepted transfer of power to wiser stewardship

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Glinda asks the Scarecrow what will become of him.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Scarecrow's future is named, and his self-doubt peeks through when he asks, "Am I really wonderful?"
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The Scarecrow tries to stand like a ruler, but you know how quickly his courage can turn back into a joke at his own expense. The question he asks Glinda is light on its face and tender underneath.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you meet his uncertainty?

options.label: A
options.text: You look at him as if the answer has been plain since the day you met.
options.outcome: You do not rush to interrupt, but your face answers before your tongue does. The Scarecrow sees in your eyes that this is no new discovery to you, no honor newly bestowed by a ruby throne. He has been wonderful all along in the crooked, practical way of dear companions who keep saving the day while apologizing for themselves. That look heartens him more than flattery would have done. His grin comes easier, and the jest in the room lands on a firmer floor of love.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into quiet affection and long-earned recognition.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: affirmed Scarecrow's worth

options.world_noticed_signal: The Scarecrow's painted smile seems less painted for a moment.

options.label: B
options.text: You speak up at once and tell him he has always been more than wonderful.
options.outcome: The answer bursts from you before the room can turn his question into too much laughter. You tell him plainly that he has always been clever, brave, and useful, whether or not anyone stuffed brains into his head. Such direct praise embarrasses him a little, but it also braces him. The courtly exchange remains funny, yet now it rests upon certainty instead of doubt. Glinda's own reply feels less like a correction and more like a coronation of what you have long known.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes bright, reassuring, and openly supportive.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: defended Scarecrow's dignity

options.world_noticed_signal: Glinda's eyes flick toward you with approval before she answers him.

options.label: C
options.text: You laugh softly with him so the question can stay sweet instead of sad.
options.outcome: You meet his uncertainty by refusing to let it sink into heaviness. A little laugh escapes you, fond and warm, and it tells him that he is safe with you even in his foolishness. The question remains tender, but no longer threatening. He can be absurd and beloved at once. The room answers in kind, and the fairy-tale strangeness of kingdoms and compliments grows gentler around him. Sometimes protection is not a shield but a hand that keeps sorrow from taking itself too seriously.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns playful, intimate, and lightly buoyant.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: softened Scarecrow's self-doubt with affection

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Glinda turns to the Tin Woodman and then the Lion.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Lion's future is named: a grand old forest, beasts awaiting their king, and the last command soon to be spent.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The Lion speaks of kingship with all the shyness of someone still half afraid he has not earned it. Yet the forest is waiting for him.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling rises first as you imagine him there?

options.label: A
options.text: You feel a sudden proud delight that makes his crown seem already real.
options.outcome: The picture of it catches you by surprise and lifts your heart in spite of everything: the great shaggy creature who once trembled at his own shadow returning at last to a forest full of beasts who will look up and know their king. Pride comes first, clean and bright. It does not erase the ache, but for one instant it outruns it. The Lion sees that light in you and stands a little taller, as if your belief reaches him sooner than the crown does.
options.tonal_effect: Narration brightens into celebratory affection.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: inwardly endorsed Lion's kingship

options.world_noticed_signal: The Lion lifts his mane with a bashful grandeur that was not quite there before.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel tender because even as a king he will still be your dear frightened friend.
options.outcome: What moves you most is not the grandeur of the forest but the old dear creature beneath it. Even in your mind's eye, with beasts bowing and branches high above him, he is still the Lion who came to you ashamed and hopeful and wanting courage like a child wants praise. The tenderness of that truth warms the whole scene. His future is noble, yes, but it is made lovelier because you know how hard won it is.
options.tonal_effect: Narration settles into affectionate, intimate warmth.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: held Lion's vulnerability with tenderness

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You feel relieved that the world has finally found the right place for him.
options.outcome: Relief comes over you with a quiet steadiness. So much of this journey has been a matter of things being misplaced: a girl in the wrong country, a ruler in a cornfield, a heart in search of its body, courage in search of its proof. To think of the Lion restored to the forest that needs him feels proper in the deepest sense. The world is not perfect yet, but one of its great confusions is being set right. That thought eases you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes orderly, assured, and gently restorative.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: recognized Lion's rightful restoration

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The breath beat lands with Scarecrow's exchange and the courtly absurdity of kingdoms.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Scarecrow asks, "Am I really wonderful?" and Glinda replies, "You are unusual."
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The answer is so solemn and so funny that the whole ruby chamber seems to smile without breaking its manners. For a little while, fairy-tale business becomes a kind of play.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you receive the absurd grace of the moment?

options.label: A
options.text: You let yourself simply enjoy how odd and lovely all of this is.
options.outcome: You do not need to add anything. The strangeness itself is enough: a scarecrow being certified unusual by a witch on a ruby throne, kingdoms assigned with perfect seriousness, and all of it somehow kinder than nonsense has any right to be. Delight steals over you softly. For one blessed minute you are not thinking only of parting or of Kansas, but of the marvelous way this country has always managed to be ridiculous and dear at once. The laughter, when it comes, feels like a reward honestly earned.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes airy, wonder-struck, and gently amused.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: took joy in Oz's absurd wonder

options.world_noticed_signal: The ruby light seems to dance more lightly over the floor.

options.label: B
options.text: You laugh aloud because the truth has never sounded so funny and so exact.
options.outcome: The laugh escapes you before ceremony can catch it, and once it does, the whole exchange grows twice as sweet. Unusual indeed! Nothing could be truer, and nothing could sound more grandly insufficient for the dear straw person who has come so far. Your laughter does not mock him; it crowns him. The chamber feels warmer for it, and even sorrow gives way for a moment under the bright little rebellion of joy.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns lively, sparkling, and socially warm.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: celebrated Scarecrow through laughter

options.world_noticed_signal: Even the solemn room cannot keep from brightening around your laugh.

options.label: C
options.text: You smile and keep the moment gentle, as if it were something to set carefully down.
options.outcome: You smile rather than laugh, for the moment feels delicate to you, like a bright small dish that might be chipped by too rough a hand. The humor remains, but it is held tenderly. Scarecrow's question, Glinda's answer, the whole strange dignity of the court: you let it stay poised between jest and blessing. The reward of the moment is not noise but sweetness, and it rests quietly in the room.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes poised, tender, and ceremonially light.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: received fairy-tale wonder with composure

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Dorothy presses Glinda to explain how she herself may reach Kansas.

emotional_choices.source_moment: After the revelation of the Silver Shoes, the companions try to comfort Dorothy by explaining what the journey gave them.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Their gratitude does not silence your ache; it only stands beside it. The room waits to see what shape your heart will take around the new truth.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you let the feeling move through you before you answer?

options.label: A
options.text: You swallow hard and try to be brave for their sakes.
options.outcome: You feel the tears coming and attempt, as children often do, to put good order over them. The companions have spoken out of love, and you do not wish to wound them by seeming to regret them. So you stand as still as you can, swallow once, and make room inside yourself for composure. The effort is touching precisely because it is not complete. Your bravery shows, but so does the child beneath it. The room grows gentler around that effort.
options.tonal_effect: Narration emphasizes courage under visible strain.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: attempted composure under grief

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: B
options.text: You let the tears come because the truth is too large for neatness.
options.outcome: You do not try to sort the feeling before it shows itself. Tears come, and with them relief of a painful kind, as if the body knows before the mind how to make room for too much truth at once. No one mistakes the tears for ingratitude. If anything, they make the companions' love feel safer, because here at last is the full measure of what the road has cost and given. The scene becomes more human and less ceremonial for a little while.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens into emotional honesty and permeability.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: allowed grief to be visible

options.world_noticed_signal: The Tin Woodman weeps with less restraint once he sees you do the same.

options.label: C
options.text: You turn a little aside as if you need one breath alone before speaking.
options.outcome: The revelation has struck too close and too strangely for immediate words. You turn aside, not in anger exactly, but in self-protection, and take one small breath as if you must step out of the room without moving your feet. The pause says what speech cannot: that wonder has limits, that love can still wound by arriving late, that home has been hurting elsewhere all this time. When you face them again, your answer carries more weight for having been earned in silence.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows inward, hushed, and edged with shock.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: withdrew briefly to contain revelation

options.world_noticed_signal: The companions fall quiet at once, as if your turned shoulder were a bell they all heard.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Glinda explains the Shoes' exact rule: three steps, any place in the world, in the wink of an eye.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — before Dorothy asks Glinda for the way home; vulnerability before authority.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Before you speak, the ruby throne seems very high, and your own wish very small only because it matters so much.

options.label: A
options.text: You lift your chin and meet Glinda's eyes.
options.narration_adjustment: You look at her as plainly as you can. If help is to be asked, it will be asked awake and face to face.

options.label: B
options.text: You glance toward your friends before speaking.
options.narration_adjustment: Your eyes go first to those who brought you here. The sight of them steadies the words in your throat.

options.label: C
options.text: You look down at the Cap in your hands.
options.narration_adjustment: The gold in your hands feels suddenly heavier than before. You begin from the burden, because it is impossible not to.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — Dorothy names Aunt Em and Uncle Henry aloud in Glinda's court.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Kansas enters the ruby room only when you let their names cross your mouth.

options.label: A
options.text: You say Aunt Em's name softly and plainly.
options.narration_adjustment: The name comes out small and dear. At once the bright country around you seems less able to keep out the gray one you love.

options.label: B
options.text: You speak briskly, as if facts will keep tears back.
options.narration_adjustment: You make it sound practical: mourning costs money, crops have failed, time matters. The plainness is its own kind of ache.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — Glinda names separate futures for Scarecrow and Tin Woodman.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: As kingdoms are handed out, your heart begins counting not crowns but distances.

options.label: A
options.text: You notice how proud they try to look.
options.narration_adjustment: You see the brave faces they put on for one another. Pride is there, and it is real, though sorrow keeps close beside it.

options.label: B
options.text: You notice how each future takes them from you.
options.narration_adjustment: Each promise sounds also like a leaving. The more happily Glinda speaks, the more final it begins to feel.

options.label: C
options.text: You notice how right each place seems.
options.narration_adjustment: Something in you recognizes fitness even before it welcomes it. These endings have the look of things being put back where they belong.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — before Dorothy presses about her own return while others are being settled.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Their futures are being arranged with such calm that your own unanswered question begins to knock harder inside you.

options.label: A
options.text: You hold your tongue for one more moment.
options.narration_adjustment: You wait, though waiting costs you. Courtesy becomes a kind of courage in its own right.

options.label: B
options.text: You lean forward as if to interrupt.
options.narration_adjustment: Your body nearly speaks before your mouth does. Home is too near now to be spoken around for long.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — the courtly absurdity of unusual rulers, monkey flights, and solemn compliments.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: For a blessed instant, Oz is not asking anything of you except that you notice how strange and sweet it has always been.

options.label: A
options.text: You let yourself grin at the whole affair.
options.narration_adjustment: The grin comes before permission. It feels good to belong, even for a moment, to a world that can be this ridiculous with a straight face.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep the smile small and careful.
options.narration_adjustment: You accept the gift of the moment without spending too much of yourself on it. Joy sits lightly, because sorrow is waiting nearby.

options.label: C
options.text: You stay solemn despite the joke.
options.narration_adjustment: The humor reaches you, but not far. Kansas is still unanswered, and your heart refuses to wander far from that fact.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Breath — Dorothy breaks the pleasant pause and reminds Glinda she still has not been told how to get home.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The laughter fades, and your wish comes back with the old sharpness, as if it had only been waiting politely in the corner.

options.label: A
options.text: You ask gently, but you ask at once.
options.narration_adjustment: Your voice is courteous, yet it does not wander. You have been patient enough to earn directness.

options.label: B
options.text: You blurt the question before ceremony can return.
options.narration_adjustment: The need breaks through all by itself. It is not graceful, but it is true, and no one can doubt what matters most to you.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Breath / early Twist — Glinda reveals the Silver Shoes could always have taken Dorothy home.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The words strike not your ears first, but the whole road behind you.

options.label: A
options.text: You go still as if the room has tilted.
options.narration_adjustment: Everything in you draws inward at once. The ruby chamber remains bright, but the brightness no longer reaches as far.

options.label: B
options.text: You stare at the Shoes as if seeing them anew.
options.narration_adjustment: Your eyes drop at once to the silver upon your feet. They have been with you so long that their treachery feels almost intimate.

options.label: C
options.text: You look straight back at Glinda.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not hide from the speaker of the blow. If this is truth, you mean to receive it from the face that bears it.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Twist — after the companions explain what they gained from the journey.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Their love rushes to meet your hurt, but it cannot decide for you what the hurt means.

options.label: A
options.text: You let their words land before answering.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not seize the first feeling and call it final. Their gratitude has earned at least a hearing inside your pain.

options.label: B
options.text: You hold fast to Aunt Em in your mind.
options.narration_adjustment: Kansas does not retreat because Oz speaks beautifully. The gray farm stays before you with stubborn, rightful force.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Resolution — Dorothy begins saying goodbye to Lion, Tin Woodman, and Scarecrow.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Now that the way is known, every second you linger becomes a kindness or a postponement, depending on how you bear it.

options.label: A
options.text: You make each goodbye slow and gentle.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not hurry the sorrow. Each friend receives the full weight of being loved before being left.

options.label: B
options.text: You move quickly before courage can fail.
options.narration_adjustment: You know too well that lingering can undo you. So you keep the parting moving, not from coldness but from fear of breaking open.

options.label: C
options.text: You pause longest with the one who looks weakest.
options.narration_adjustment: Your kindness goes where it feels most needed in the moment. Love, even now, chooses by ache as much as by fairness.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Benevolent, composed, discerning, evaluating Dorothy's maturity.
Affectionate, uneasy about parting, eager for Dorothy's success.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Glinda knows the powers of the Golden Cap and Silver Shoes; Dorothy does not yet know the Shoes' homeward power; companions know only their own histories and Glinda's visible authority.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Immediate hope under ceremonial tension; the price of help is surrender.
canon_boundaries_active: The Golden Cap must be surrendered to Glinda as condition of help.
No one but Glinda may yet reveal the Silver Shoes' true power.
Glinda remains kind but firm; she does not waive the exchange.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to keep or negotiate away transfer of the Cap entirely.
Player asks Glinda to send the Winged Monkeys to Kansas.
Player attempts to activate the Shoes before revelation.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Glinda knows and announces the three final uses of the Cap and later the Shoes' power; companions know their own hopes and react from lived transformation; Dorothy learns the truth only when Glinda says it.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: The company is being lovingly dismantled; humor briefly relieves the ache before revelation reopens it at greater depth.
canon_boundaries_active: Glinda's three commands must go to Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion.
The Monkey King's future freedom remains part of Glinda's plan.
The Silver Shoes must be revealed as the true route home.
The journey's meaning may be interpreted, but Dorothy still chooses home.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to redirect one of the final commands away from canon destinations.
Player demands that companions accompany Dorothy to Kansas.
Player rejects the truth of the Shoes outright or attempts to stay in Oz permanently.

present_npcs.current_disposition: Sorrowful, loving, trying to be brave.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Everyone present knows how the Shoes work; only Dorothy can decide the inward promise that frames her departure; Kansas itself remains offstage.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: The practical problem is solved, but the emotional act of leaving is not; this is a threshold scene.
canon_boundaries_active: Dorothy must take Toto in her arms before departure.
The Shoes require three heel-clicks and a spoken destination.
The session ends before the transport to Kansas resolves.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to delay indefinitely or remain in Oz.
Player tries to take companions physically to Kansas.
Player attempts to use the Shoes for any destination other than home at this ending beat.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Glinda does not extend her hand at once; she answers Dorothy's question in full, counting the three remaining commands on her fingers—'one for the Scarecrow, one for the Tin Woodman, one for the Lion'—and then adds, before the whole court, 'after that, the Monkeys shall be free.'
paths.current_session_echo: In the command-allocation scene, the Monkey King's freedom has already been spoken aloud, so the chamber goes still with approval rather than surprise when Glinda later names liberation as the Cap's final destination.
paths.next_session_payoff: When the Monkey King receives the Cap in Kansas-adjacent recap framing, he bows first to Dorothy and says, 'You asked what would become of us before you let us go; I have not forgotten that.'
paths.later_session_legacy: In end-state summary text, Dorothy is framed as a keeper of power who required public fairness before surrender, not as a child simply relieved of a burden.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not cling to power, but you refuse to hand it over asleep.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Glinda publicly explains the exact use of all three remaining commands before taking the Cap, and the Monkey King's eventual freedom is named in the room.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Direct dialogue from Glinda, companions leaning in to listen, and a visible settling of tension in the ruby chamber when 'free' is spoken.

world_state_delta.inventory: Golden Cap ownership transfers from Dorothy to Glinda.

npc_shifts.direction: respect for prudence and moral seriousness increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: asked for just use before surrendering power

resets: The pause before transfer and the ceremonial silence of Glinda's court clear once Dorothy has left Oz.
persists: Golden Cap no longer belongs to Dorothy.
Glinda's reading of Dorothy as fair-minded with power persists into homecoming moral framing.
Monkey King's memory that Dorothy asked after the Monkeys' fate persists.
escalates: If paired with other balanced attachment choices, narrator increasingly frames Dorothy as morally exact rather than merely innocent.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: The instant the Cap leaves Dorothy's hands, Glinda's face warms; she rises from the ruby throne instead of remaining seated and says, 'You have done a clean thing quickly,' before turning at once to the Scarecrow's future.
paths.current_session_echo: In the companion-allocation scene, the Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion each answer more readily, as if Dorothy's swift surrender has made the parting feel orderly rather than contested.
paths.next_session_payoff: At the Kansas opening recap, Glinda's remembered farewell line becomes, 'She trusted me before she knew the whole of it,' coloring Dorothy's return as an act of disciplined generosity rather than reluctant loss.
paths.later_session_legacy: In final moral accounting, Dorothy's surrender of the Cap is cited alongside missing the balloon for Toto as proof that she can relinquish advantage without bargaining.
paths.defining_line_captured: Some powers grow lighter only when you stop owning them.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Glinda responds with immediate physical warmth and the courtly redistribution proceeds without any explanatory pause or friction.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Glinda stands, smiles openly, speaks a line of approval, and the companions draw easier breaths instead of waiting tensely.

world_state_delta.inventory: Golden Cap ownership transfers from Dorothy to Glinda.
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: surrendered the Golden Cap freely

resets: Glinda's standing from the throne and the immediate courtly momentum are scene-bound and clear after departure.
persists: Golden Cap no longer belongs to Dorothy.
Glinda's affectionate trust persists into retrospective narration.
Companions remember Dorothy as someone who did not make the last exchange harder than it had to be.
escalates: Repeated lawful relinquishment across the story compounds into a stronger whole-duty homecoming frame.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: When Dorothy admits she hates to lose the last thing in Oz that obeys her, the room stills; Glinda's smile disappears, and she receives the Cap with both hands and the line, 'Many call command safety until they must live without it.'
paths.current_session_echo: In the next exchange about the companions' futures, Glinda's tone becomes more teacherly than tender, and the Scarecrow watches Dorothy instead of the throne, as if checking whether the surrender truly happened.
paths.next_session_payoff: In the opening recap of Kansas return, the remembered Glinda line is the instructive one—'Many call command safety'—so Dorothy's homecoming is shaded by the lesson of giving up mastery rather than by pure relief.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final summary text can explicitly contrast Dorothy's honesty about wanting control with her actual act of relinquishing it, preserving moral complexity rather than flattening her into simple generosity.
paths.defining_line_captured: You give it up, but not without letting the room hear the cost.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Glinda becomes visibly more formal and frames the transfer as a moral lesson instead of a warm exchange.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Glinda's expression cools, her dialogue turns aphoristic, and the companions stop moving for a beat before the ceremony resumes.

world_state_delta.inventory: Golden Cap ownership transfers from Dorothy to Glinda.
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: admitted attachment to power before surrendering it

resets: The chamber's sudden stillness and Glinda's ceremonial pause end with the scene.
persists: Golden Cap no longer belongs to Dorothy.
Glinda's reading of Dorothy as honest but still learning to release power persists.
Scarecrow's protective attention to Dorothy's distress persists into farewell shading.
escalates: If combined with other clinging or grief-forward choices, the narrator increasingly frames Dorothy as paying visibly for every letting-go.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Glinda stops mid-distribution after Dorothy blurts that she wishes they could all come with her; the Lion's eyes fill at once, the Tin Woodman presses his axe tight beneath his arm, and the Scarecrow says nothing for a full beat.
paths.current_session_echo: During the farewell sequence, each goodbye takes longer: the Lion nuzzles Dorothy before speaking, the Tin Woodman must wipe his face, and even Glinda allows the silence to stand instead of moving briskly onward.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 7 opens with Aunt Em's name arriving through tears before the prairie is described, because Dorothy's last Oz-moment was not composure but the refusal to pretend parting felt proper.
paths.later_session_legacy: In retrospective narration, Oz remains emotionally near and difficult to set down; memory is described as fresh hurt rather than polished keepsake.
paths.defining_line_captured: You make the room admit that happy endings can still feel like being torn apart.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The formal ceremony is interrupted by visible grief from all three companions and a pause from Glinda.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Glinda halts, the Lion's eyes wet, the Tin Woodman clutches his axe, and the room's grand tone collapses into raw feeling.

npc_shifts.direction: perception of Dorothy as reluctant to release increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: wished the company could remain together

resets: Glinda's ceremonial pause and the immediate hush in the chamber clear once the farewells are over.
persists: Companions remember Dorothy as openly unwilling to make the parting neat.
Farewell emotional temperature remains high into the heel-click decision.
escalates: Repeated attachment-forward choices compound into a homecoming that feels more torn between worlds.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: As each future is named, Dorothy answers with a specific memory—the pole, the forest, the road—and the companions visibly straighten, because they are being remembered in particulars rather than blessed in general.
paths.current_session_echo: In the goodbye scene, each companion answers with his own remembered image in return: the Scarecrow mentions the cornfield, the Tin Woodman the oil-can, and the Lion the poppy field rescue.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 7 opens on Kansas with memory arriving before explanation: the first inward image is not a throne or a spell but one precise companion detail, proving Dorothy kept the promise of exact remembrance.
paths.later_session_legacy: Retrospective text can call back named shared moments rather than generic friendship, making Oz survive in Dorothy through concrete recollection.
paths.defining_line_captured: You cannot keep them near, so you make memory do the work of distance.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The farewell tone becomes reciprocal remembrance, with each companion receiving a named memory instead of a protest or a release blessing.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Dorothy names specific past scenes and the companions answer with softened faces and steadier posture.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: promised lasting remembrance to companions

resets: The scene-specific exchange of named memories ends when transport begins.
persists: Promise of remembrance persists as Dorothy's final inward bond to Oz.
Companions' sense of being remembered specifically persists in recap and ending text.
escalates: Balanced attachment choices accumulate into a reflective rather than torn homecoming voice.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: When Dorothy blesses each companion's separate country, Glinda gives a small approving nod, and the Lion lifts his head, the Scarecrow folds his hands behind him like a ruler, and the Tin Woodman settles his axe as if already carrying state rather than travel.
paths.current_session_echo: In the farewell sequence, the companions speak more like future sovereigns than bereaved travelers: the Scarecrow promises wise rule, the Tin Woodman promises kindness to the Winkies, and the Lion speaks of his forest as a duty he can now meet.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 7 opens with Kansas described in plain, rightful terms—earth, fence, wind—because Dorothy ended Oz by helping everyone stand in the places meant for them, including herself.
paths.later_session_legacy: End-state narration frames Dorothy as one who could love without asking the road to reverse itself, strengthening the moral order of the ending.
paths.defining_line_captured: You love them enough to stop asking the road to keep what it has finished making.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The companions begin inhabiting their future roles immediately, and Glinda's ceremony regains stately momentum.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Glinda nods approval, the Lion physically lifts his head, and each companion's posture shifts toward rulership.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: accepted the company breaking into separate futures

resets: The courtly posture of the farewell ceremony clears once Dorothy is gone from Oz.
persists: Companions remember Dorothy as having released them cleanly into their callings.
Glinda's approval of Dorothy's mature release persists.
escalates: Repeated release-forward choices compound into a firmer, less divided Kansas return.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: After Glinda reveals the Shoes' power, Dorothy answers by naming what the journey gave her friends; the Scarecrow's face lifts first, then the Tin Woodman gives a broken grateful smile, and the Lion lets out the breath he was holding.
paths.current_session_echo: In the farewell sequence, the companions speak from gratitude rather than apology: the Scarecrow thanks Dorothy for making him useful, the Tin Woodman blesses her tenderly, and the Lion's sorrow is steadied by pride.
paths.next_session_payoff: Kansas opens under a softened moral light: before Dorothy sees Aunt Em clearly, the inward narration admits pain but refuses to call Oz a mistake, preserving the journey as costly good.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final summary text can explicitly frame Dorothy as someone who learned to bless what hurt her without pretending it was harmless.
paths.defining_line_captured: You refuse to call the road a mistake while your friends are standing in what it made of them.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The companions are visibly comforted by Dorothy's interpretation, and the room brightens emotionally instead of sinking into guilt.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Faces lift, breath releases, and companion replies come with gratitude rather than stricken silence.

world_state_delta.inventory: Silver Shoes remain worn by Dorothy; knowledge_of_power updates.

npc_shifts.direction: perception of Dorothy as matured through gratitude increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: interpreted suffering through gratitude

resets: The immediate brightening of the ruby chamber is scene-bound.
persists: Silver Shoes are now known as homeward power.
Dorothy's interpretation of the journey as worthwhile despite sorrow persists into homecoming narration.
Glinda's mature reading of Dorothy persists.
escalates: If paired with other lawful-release choices, homecoming narration becomes increasingly serene and morally integrated.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy names Aunt Em's wasted sorrow without softening it; the Scarecrow lowers his head, the Tin Woodman begins to weep in earnest, and no one interrupts her until Glinda answers.
paths.current_session_echo: The farewell scene carries a shadow of guilt: the companions cling more tenderly, speak more quietly, and their blessings toward Dorothy are edged with the knowledge that her home suffered for their becoming.
paths.next_session_payoff: Kansas opens with urgency and ache: the first image is gray yard and worn hands before wonder has any place, because Dorothy carried homeward not balance but the full moral bruise of delay.
paths.later_session_legacy: Retrospective narration preserves Oz as beloved but morally expensive, preventing the ending from smoothing over Aunt Em's absence and grief.
paths.defining_line_captured: You will not let wonder excuse the wound it left at home.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The chamber deepens into sober sorrow, and the companions show visible guilt instead of relief.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Heads lower, tears begin, tails and hands still, and Glinda allows the silence rather than rushing to comfort.

world_state_delta.inventory: Silver Shoes remain worn by Dorothy; knowledge_of_power updates.

npc_shifts.direction: solemn respect for truthfulness under pain increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: reckoned with time lost to Aunt Em's suffering

resets: The chamber's immediate hush and companion silence end with the scene.
persists: Silver Shoes are now known as homeward power.
Dorothy's grief-first interpretation of the journey persists into Kansas narration.
Companions' farewell tenderness remains guilt-colored.
escalates: If combined with earlier grief- or attachment-forward choices, homecoming becomes increasingly raw and difficult to narrate as simple reward.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy holds gratitude and cost together, and the room steadies rather than swings; Glinda's gaze lingers, the Scarecrow meets Dorothy's eyes again, and the Tin Woodman stops weeping long enough to listen.
paths.current_session_echo: In the farewell scene, both joy and sorrow remain active: companions bless Dorothy warmly, but no one pretends the parting or the delay was painless.
paths.next_session_payoff: Kansas opens in reflective equilibrium: plain earth, relief, and the ache of Oz arrive in the same breath, because Dorothy chose not to erase either truth.
paths.later_session_legacy: End-state narration can present Dorothy as morally clearer than when she arrived in Oz—not because she became harder, but because she can hold contradiction without fleeing it.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the journey keep its gift and its blame.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The scene settles into balanced reflection instead of bright gratitude or dark grief.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Glinda studies Dorothy longer, companions relax without smiling broadly, and the room's emotional temperature stabilizes.

world_state_delta.inventory: Silver Shoes remain worn by Dorothy; knowledge_of_power updates.

npc_shifts.direction: deep approval for mature balance increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: held gratitude and grief together

resets: The room's poised stillness is scene-specific and ends at transport.
persists: Silver Shoes are now known as homeward power.
Glinda's strongest approval of Dorothy's balance persists into retrospective framing.
Dorothy's homecoming voice remains double-toned: relieved and bereft together.
escalates: Repeated balanced choices across the story compound into a mature, measured narrator voice at the ending.

First character: Aunt Em, running before she fully understands what she sees. Immediate stakes: Dorothy must choose whether to speak Oz first as fact, memory, or impossible dream.
paths.later_session_legacy: Oz remains narratively alive inside Kansas scenes through concrete callbacks rather than abstract longing; later recaps can surface remembered details as living presences.

paths.next_session_payoff: In Kansas, Dorothy runs to Aunt Em without pausing to test whether Oz can be held beside the prairie; the reunion lands as full recommitment before reflection begins.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: plain, clean, almost stern relief. First image: hard Kansas ground under Dorothy's shoes, with Oz's colors gone at once. paths.defining_line_captured: You do not merely leave Oz; you return yourself to Kansas.
